title: Global Product Management Intern E&C Large
description_html:
SUMMER 2026

Shure offers a challenging, fun and rewarding summer internship program. The twelve-week program is offered to undergraduate and graduate students. We offer internships with a variety of work arrangements from onsite interns to fully remote in US. Each intern will receive a competitive salary. Additionally, Interns who are asked to relocate to Illinois for onsite internships will receive a housing stipend to cover living expenses. Applications will be collected, reviewed, and selected candidates will be contacted in late fall/early winter.

We are looking for an enthusiastic, driven intern to join our Global Product Management team. The Global Product Management Intern , E&C Large, will assist with product development and lifecycle management activities. To be a successful product management intern, you must combine strong analytical skills with a passion for expanding product knowledge. Responsibilities include documenting and executing internal company processes, assisting in the development of business plans for the high-tier wireless category, and supporting various product testing activities.

This Internship is Onsite or Hybrid, based in our Niles, IL, location.

Responsibilities

  • Represents the Global Marketing and Sales Division during product development projects as the “voice of the customer” ensuring that product attributes will create a positive end user value proposition.
  • Works alongside product managers to execute logistical planning for Alpha and Beta tests with end users.
  • Analyze competitive products such as wired and wireless microphones, in-ear monitors, earphones, and audio accessories.
  • Reviews user documentation for accuracy regarding product features, proper usage and expected performance.
  • Analyze sales and market data to facilitate business decisions for both existing Shure products and new product concepts.
  • Performs other related duties as requested, such as taking notes for Global Product Management and other region-specific meetings, purchase orders, department shipping needs, etc.

Qualifications

  • Currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in marketing, business, music technology or sound engineering and is junior level standing or higher
  • Demonstrated knowledge of Pro-Audio networks is ideal (microphones, lighting, speakers, mixing consoles, video equipment or other live production equipment)
  • Excellent written and verbal communication
  • Excellent listening skills with the ability to interface with a variety of audiences
  • Analytical thinker with the ability to offer recommendations based on data and customer feedback
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Products (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, etc.)
  • Interest in audio, music, live performance, technology, and computer networking are a plus

Applicants for this position must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Shure will not sponsor applicants for this position for work visas.

Who We Are

Shure’s mission is to be the most trusted audio brand worldwide – and for over a century, our Core Values have aligned us to be just that. Founded in 1925, we are a leading global manufacturer of audio equipment known for quality, reliability, and durability. We engineer microphones, headphones, wireless audio systems, conferencing systems, and more. And quality doesn’t stop at our products. Our talented teams strive for perfection and innovate every chance they get. We offer an Associate-first culture, flexible work arrangements, and opportunity for all.

Shure is headquartered in United States. We have more than 35 regional sales offices, engineering hubs, distribution centers and manufacturing facilities throughout the Americas, EMEA, and Asia.

Take the Stage and Amplify Your Impact. Explore our unique culture and learn the key pillars that define our legacy of excellence.

THE MIX MATTERS

Don’t check off every box in the job requirements? No problem! We recognize that every professional journey is unique and are committed to providing an equitable candidate experience for all prospective Shure Associates. If you’re excited about this role, believe you’ve got the skills to be successful, and share our passion for creating an inclusive, diverse, equitable, and accessible work environment, then apply!

PAY TRANSPARENCY

Modeled by extensive market analysis and economic best practices, Shure offers competitive intern compensation that is reviewed and adjusted annually by our Total Rewards Team to attract, hire, and retain the industry’s top intern talent. Each internship has an hourly rate which varies from $21-$40 per hour based on job function (Engineering, IT, Business, etc) and year completed in school (Junior, Masters 2nd year, PHD, etc).

WE GOT YOU - Our Benefits

At Shure, we prioritize the well-being of our associates. Benefits for our interns include retirement savings plans and paid time off, employee discounts, professional development opportunities, and work-life balance initiatives. To learn more, check out our Intern Benefits At A Glance

title: ESPN Chicago Summer 2026 Content Internship
description_html:
ESPN Chicago Summer 2026 Content Internship


(CHICAGO, IL)


ESPN Chicago at Good Karma Brands is currently recruiting college students & recent college graduates for the 2026 Summer Internship Program. This is a part-time paid internship that will provide an excellent opportunity to learn more about sport marketing and media. The ideal candidate will be an organized, self-starter who is interested in hands-on experience in the areas of sports content creation, promotional event marketing, and new business development. ESPN Chicago is a sports marketing company with assets including ESPN 1000, Chicago Bears Radio Network, Hard Rock Casino White Sox Radio Network, and local digital display and video inventory on ESPN.com.


GKB is a media and marketing company with expertise in leveraging the power of national and local sports and news to build brands and connect with fans through audio, video, digital and events. With offices throughout the country and our home office in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, GKB’s team includes over 550 teammates and is recognized as a “Best Place to Work” by Front Office Sports, Sports Business Journal, and USA TODAY. Take a look at Good Karma Brands here.


Daily Responsibilities of an intern:



  • Take initiative to learn all aspects of the business, ideally becoming competent in GKB’s content creation strategies best practices (on-air and online), marketing execution, and the GKB partner acquisition process 

  • Primarily assist with the Content department with social content creation, video and sound editing, and show prep

  • Collaborate with the Marketing department on event planning, set-up and execution, and event breakdown

  • Engage and interact with local partners and fans during events

  • Other assigned tasks

  • Be a GKB ambassador by adhering to the GKB core values: Be Honest, Work Hard, Over Deliver, Listen, Teamwork and Follow Through. 


Hours and Pay


This internship is approximately 25–30 hours/week from June to August 2026. Interns are paid $16.60 an hour and will gain experience in marketing, sales, and content.


How to Apply 


Apply by submitting your resume to www.goodkarmabrands.com/careers no later than Friday, March 20th. All are encouraged to apply. 


About Good Karma Brands


As a proud partner of ESPN, GKB represents the ESPN Radio Network and Podcasts, the full portfolio of ESPN Digital Products, ESPN Events and local ESPN affiliated radio stations in eight markets including New York, Los Angeles and Chicago. GKB’s other audio assets include Newsradio 620 WTMJ and 101.7 The Truth in Milwaukee, and 95.3 WBEV in Beaver Dam, WI.   


With offices throughout the country and our home office in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, GKB’s team includes over 550 teammates and was recognized as a “Best Place to Work” by multiple organizations, including Front Office Sports.  


For a full list of assets and more information, please visit www.goodkarmabrands.com. 


 


##


Good Karma Brands is an Equal Opportunity Employer.  We consistently renew our commitment to creating and maintaining an inclusive workplace for all.  We foster integrity, respect within the workplace, and believe that the foundation of our success is our collective differences and diversity of thought.  We welcome all to help us continue to build a community that embodies and promotes diversity, inclusion, and belonging for all.

title: Audio News Desk Intern
description_html:
About Chicago Public Media

Chicago Public Media is one of the nation’s largest local nonprofit news organizations. Home to WBEZ 91.5FM, the Chicago Sun-Times, and Vocalo. Chicago Public Media reaches more than 3.3 million people weekly across broadcast, print, and digital platforms. As a mission-driven organization, we aspire to be the most trusted news source Chicagoans turn to for understanding the people, events, and ideas shaping our community.

Chicago Public Media is a 100% community-funded newsroom powered by nearly 97,000 members whose support makes our journalism possible. This sustained community investment helped drive nationally recognized work in 2025, including 29 awards from esteemed organizations, including the Public Media Journalists Association, the Edward R. Murrow Awards, and the National Association of Black Journalists.

We believe independent journalism is essential to a healthy democracy and that access to objective, fact-based news is a right for every citizen. We serve the public interest by creating diverse content that informs and enriches our community, supported by a dedicated staff of approximately 250 professionals.

For more information, please see the Chicago Public Media Annual Report.

The Opportunity

We are looking for an Audio Intern for our newsroom. The WBEZ audio intern will be available to help with daily news and production needs to support the work we do both for broadcast and on-demand. Our audio news intern will work across the newsroom with other reporters and editors. Interns learn to write audio and digital news, produce multimedia content, record field audio, edit audio, and they learn other skills required by daily reporters and producers at radio stations. Ideal candidates have strong collaboration and communication skills, as well as some previous work as a reporter. They show a natural curiosity about local news and bring fresh perspectives to listeners and readers. They were willing to learn from experienced newsroom reporters, producers and editors.

We are located at Navy Pier in Chicago, Illinois. All interns must be able to work from our office and studios at Navy Pier in Chicago a minimum of 2 days a week.

Chicago Public Media interns work alongside some of the best talent in the business. Our hope is that — after receiving a solid foundation at WBEZ — our interns continue to contribute to public media and audio journalism.

A Bit About Our Internship Programs

  • Many, but not all, of our interns have completed college or some other form of education and/or certification in the journalism or media field(s).
  • Many go on to careers in public media or other outlets. Many have found their way back to WBEZ, too.
  • We pay $20/hour, non-negotiable

Interns work full time 40 hrs/week Program term is 12 weeks (June 9, 2026 - September 10, 2026)

  • Positions are daytime work hours on a Monday through Friday schedule, though some flexibility may be possible.

What we are looking for our candidates to display in their resumes, cover letters and interview conversations:

  • Knowledge about WBEZ and our mission of serving the community
  • Strong interest in learning and contributing to our mission
  • Curiosity and the willingness to learn
  • Understanding of the value of bringing together diverse perspectives
  • Why WBEZ is part of your career planning in journalism and media
  • Specific skills you want to learn during the internship
  • If applicable, any links to a portfolio of writing samples; audio, video, or multimedia packages; or other completed work that may be relevant to this opening

The application deadline is Friday, March 20, 2026. Managers plan to select candidates by early May.

Accepted applicants will be required to start training in Chicago on June 9, 2026. Internship ends September 10, 2026.

Working at Chicago Public Media

At Chicago Public Media, we care deeply about our employees as we know attracting, developing, and growing talent is key to our success and enhancing our impact.

Our culture is one where collaboration, diversity of ideas, and innovation are encouraged. We value colleagues who will enhance our culture by bringing new ideas, divergent experiences, and talents to our dynamic workplace.

At Chicago Public Media we believe dedication to a great workplace includes supporting our employees and their families. As a result, we provide a broad and generous benefits package for employees at hire and in the years to come.

Our benefits include a competitive salary and benefits package which includes medical, dental, vision, vacation, holidays, life insurance, disability coverage, retirement savings, and a commuter benefits plan.

Chicago Public Media is an Equal Opportunity Employer and we actively seek and welcome people from all backgrounds, orientations, and life experiences to join our team.

The essential functions described above are not all-inclusive and are not intended to create any contractual or other legal commitment. Chicago Public Media may change the content or format of this job at any time in its sole and exclusive discretion without notice.

title: Marketing Internship
description_html:
Happy Hospitality Marketing Internship 

Part-Time | Paid | On-Site (Chicago, IL)

As an intern, you'll gain hands-on experience supporting our marketing team, working across marketing, content, and brand initiatives while learning directly from industry leaders. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, love all things marketing and social media, and want the chance to collaborate and brainstorm with a creative team, this is the perfect opportunity for you! We're a rapidly growing, trendy restaurant group shaking up industry, and we're on the hunt for a creative and dynamic MarketingIntern to help us spread the word and tantalize taste buds across the digital landscape.

 

About Us:

We’re the marinara-muddled minds behind Happy Camper, Homeslice, and Paradise Park with locations in Chicago, Denver and Las Vegas. Our restaurants are wall to wall with lights, murals, and vignettes for the perfect gram. We are known for our art-inspired spaces, delicious food, and great vibes. If you ask us, the experts, there's not a better combination of pizza, booze, vibes, and happiness in the whole universe than what you'll get here.  

 

 What You'll Do:

  • Work with the Brand Manager in developing and executing advertising campaigns.
  • Partner with the marketing team on merchandise collaborations and in-restaurant marketing initiatives.
  • Assist in creating and executing exciting campaigns, promotions, and events that drive engagement and brand awareness.
  • Contribute to social media strategy and content creation, identifying new opportunities to enhance our online presence.
  • Support event nights and activations, helping with set-up, customer engagement, and on-site execution.
  • Handle day-to-day social media tasks such as influencer research, community management, and trend tracking.
  • Create engaging visual assets and Canva collages to support marketing, social, and content initiatives.
 

What We're Looking For:

  • A strong interest in marketing, advertising, and brand strategy.
  • Ability to work collaboratively in an in-person team environment.
  • A social media enthusiast who keeps up with the latest trends, platforms, and content styles.
  • Passion for the restaurant, food, and hospitality industry.
  • Strong creative and photography skills, with an eye for capturing engaging visuals.
  • A proactive, curious, and idea-driven mindset.
  • Previous experience in social media management or content creation is a plus, but not required - we're more interested in your passion and potential!
  • Must be available to work on-site in downtown Chicago 2–3 times per week.
  • Must be 21 or older due to job-related requirements involving alcohol-focused events.

Physical Demands & Work Environment

This role is primarily office-based. Occasional support of events or activations may require standing for extended periods, light lifting (up to 15 pounds), and working in environments with elevated noise levels.

Happy Hospitality will provide reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities in accordance with applicable laws.

 

Non-Physical Demands 

Frequently requires time pressures, frequent change of tasks, tedious or exacting work, and working with others as part of a team. Occasionally requires performing multiple tasks simultaneously, emergency situations, and irregular schedule. 

 

Schedule & Internship Details

Status: Internship (temporary)
Schedule: Part-time, approximately 10-15 hours per week
Location: On-site, Chicago, Illinois

Department: Marketing

Reports to: Digital Marketing Manager & Brand Manager
 

Compensation:

Hourly rate: $20

 

Benefits:

Gain hands-on experience in social media marketing and the restaurant industry

Work closely with a fun and supportive team of fellow food and good vibe enthusiasts.

Opportunity to contribute to the growth and success of multiple restaurants.

Enjoy delicious perks like dining discounts at all our locations.
 

Think you have what it takes to help us take over the marketing realm, one mouthwatering campaign at a time? Apply now! 
 

Happy Hospitality  is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity in gender, race, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, religion, age, veteran status, life experiences and ideas. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and strive to provide an inclusive culture that embraces diversity. Happy Hospitality will provide reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities in accordance with applicable laws.

 

Must be eligible to legally work in the United States.

title: LN Concerts, Marketing Intern
description_html:
Job Summary

WHO ARE WE?

Live Nation Entertainment is the world’s leading live entertainment company, comprised of global market leaders: Ticketmaster, Live Nation Concerts, and Live Nation Media & Sponsorship. Ticketmaster is the global leader in event ticketing with over 620 million tickets sold annually and approximately 10,000 clients worldwide. Live Nation Concerts is the largest provider of live entertainment in the world promoting more than 50,000 events annually for nearly 7,000 artists in 40+ countries. These businesses allow Live Nation Media & Sponsorship to create strategic music marketing programs that connect more than 1,200 sponsors with the 145 million fans that attend Live Nation Entertainment events each year. For additional information, visit www.livenationentertainment.com.

WHO ARE YOU?

Passionate and motivated. Driven, with an entrepreneurial spirit. Resourceful, innovative, forward thinking and committed. At Live Nation Entertainment, our people embrace these qualities, so if this sounds like you then please read on!

THE POSITION – MARKETING INTERN

Are you looking for an exciting internship experience? Our paid Internship Program provides real-world experience for students who are interested in the live entertainment industry. After finishing this program, you will have gained broad experience in various aspects live entertainment business. You can also expect to apply your academic learning to real-world situations and cultivate your leadership, problem-solving, and communication skills. This is a part-time position, up to 40 hours per week.

What This Role Will Do

  • Assisting marketing department with administrative duties
  • Compiling distribution list for flyers and posters
  • Research target markets for concerts
  • Research digital avenues in various markets
  • Maintaining contact list for local media
  • Research and contact influencers to use on marketing campaigns
  • Set up promotions with approved influencers
  • Final internship project will be assigned at the beginning of the internship program to be presented at the end

LEARNING OBJECTIVES

  • Learn the process of marketing concerts
  • Learning how to properly research target demographics
  • Learn how to build and maintain relationships with outside organizations

Preferred Qualifications

  • Currently enrolled in an accredited College or University; Preferably in a field related to the internship you are applying for
  • Recent Graduate or Junior or Senior standing
  • Previous experience preferred (school, internship, or professional)
  • Creative thinker and problem solver
  • Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills
  • Acute sense of judgment, tact, and diplomacy
  • A strong sense of teamwork and ability to execute programs

This program is in the Summer of 2026.

EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY

We are passionate and committed to our people and go beyond the rhetoric of diversity and inclusion. You will be working in an inclusive environment and be encouraged to bring your whole self to work. We will do all that we can to help you successfully balance your work and homelife. As a growing business we will encourage you to develop your professional and personal aspirations, enjoy new experiences, and learn from the talented people you will be working with. It’s talent that matters to us and we encourage applications from people irrespective of their gender, race, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability status or caring responsibilities.

Live Nation strongly supports equal employment opportunity for all applicants regardless of age (40 and over), ancestry, color, religious creed (including religious dress and grooming practices), family and medical care leave or the denial of family and medical care leave, mental or physical disability (including HIV and AIDS), marital status, domestic partner status, medical condition (including cancer and genetic characteristics), genetic information, military and veteran status, political affiliation, national origin (including language use restrictions), citizenship, race, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding and medical conditions related to pregnancy, childbirth or breastfeeding), gender, gender identity, and gender expression, sexual orientation, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state or local law, rule, ordinance or regulation.

We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of the Los Angeles Fair Chance Ordinance, San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act and consistent with other similar and / or applicable laws in other areas.

We also afford equal employment opportunities to qualified individuals with a disability. For this reason, Live Nation will make reasonable accommodations for the known physical or mental limitations of an otherwise qualified individual with a disability who is an applicant consistent with its legal obligations to do so, including reasonable accommodations related to pregnancy in accordance with applicable local, state and / or federal law. As part of its commitment to make reasonable accommodations, Live Nation also wishes to participate in a timely, good faith, interactive process with a disabled applicant to determine effective reasonable accommodations, if any, which can be made in response to a request for accommodations. $16.60 USD - $20.00 USD Hourly

title: Marketing Intern
description_html:
Your future position: Marketing Intern

Position Type: Part-Time (20-30 hours per week)

Choose your location: Crystal Lake or Chicago, IL

Why you should join our team! Why Baxter & Woodman?

At Baxter & Woodman, both seasoned professionals and recent graduates find more than a job; they uncover a long-lasting, fulfilling career with a distinguished leader in technology and innovation. Our work environment is extraordinary and being honored as one of the 2025 Zweig Best Firms to Work For exemplifies our appreciation and respect for our dedicated staff.

About The Role, Your Impact

Primary functions are to assist with updating marketing materials, CRM system, and various projects to support the Business Development efforts of Vice Presidents.

What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like

  • Prepare and update marketing materials in various platforms including Adobe InDesign, Microsoft Word, and PowerPoint.
  • Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and Digital Asset Management (DAM) database maintenance.
  • Support writing and organizing resumes and project descriptions.
  • Assist in coordinating with consultant firms for teaming and gathering marketing materials.
  • Assist with the planning of client entertainment events and conferences.
  • Assist with organizing and preparing marketing materials for business development efforts.
  • Support social media strategy and content creation, including brainstorming, drafting, and scheduling posts
  • Design LinkedIn banners and branded graphics for internal sharing, recruiting, and new hires
  • Assist in refreshing recruiting and employer-brand materials, ensuring visual consistency and appeal
  • Create logos, graphics, and visual assets for seasonal merchandise and promotional items
  • Help execute expanded social media campaigns, turning ideas into finished content
  • Design a custom holiday card and assist with a broader holiday marketing campaign across print and digital channels
  • Contribute creative fresh ideas and assets for Zweig Best Firm announcements and promotional efforts
  • Assist with annual company golf outings - coordination and promotion
  • Assist in planning employee appreciation events and special initiatives, including 2026 employee summer event.
    • Duties for planning the summer event include:
      • Point of contact for staff questions.
      • Assist with registrations (making sure staff register).
      • Coordinate final registration counts for activities.
      • Event email correspondence.
      • Assist with decorations, welcome bags, name badges, etc.
      • Attend weekly planning meetings.
      • On-site event support including assisting with registration, ensuring events are set up to specifications, taking photos, etc.
Education/Certification Requirements

  • Pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications or other related field of study.

Must-haves, What You Will Bring

  • Experience working in engineering or architecture a plus.
  • Knowledge of InDesign, Photoshop, and graphic design a plus.
  • Strong communication and organizational skills.
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Office.

Compensation: The salary for this role is $21.00 per hour. Actual compensation packages are based on several factors that are unique to each candidate, including but not limited to skill set, depth of experience, and certifications.

You matter! Our outstanding benefits - At our organization, we prioritize the holistic well-being of our employees through a comprehensive range of benefits. This commitment is evident in our healthcare offerings, including medical, dental, and vision plans, alongside an Employee Assistance Program and dedicated benefit advisors for personalized support. Financial well-being is enhanced through our financial planning program, life and disability insurance, tax savings plans, and coverage for accidents, and cancer. Balancing work and life, we provide generous paid time off, paid holidays, parental leave, and flexible work arrangements, including the option to work from home on half Fridays and varied schedules. Additionally, we lead the industry by offering 401k plans, supported by expert financial advisors, and an Employee Stock Ownership program, ensuring our employees are not just part of a workplace but a thriving, supportive community dedicated to their well-being, financial security, and happiness.

Baxter & Woodman is an equal opportunity - Vet/Disability employer

title: Content Marketing Intern
description_html:
We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Content Marketing Intern to join our marketing team. This internship offers an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in content creation, marketing, and brand communication and design.

The primary responsibility is to assist in developing high-quality, engaging content for various marketing channels. This includes working on content-related projects, auditing existing content, assisting in the production of email marketing campaigns, and writing short-form copy. In addition, the Content Marketing Intern will contribute to the planning, scheduling and management of social media content across platforms such as Instagram, LinkedIn, and Pinterest. This includes crafting compelling posts, engaging with followers, and monitoring social media trends. The role will also involve researching content marketing trends, emerging social media platforms, and industry best practices.

The ideal candidate will have strong writing skills and a passion for storytelling across digital platforms. Familiarity with tools like Adobe Creative Suite and Canva is a plus, but not required.

Qualifications:

  • Strong interest in content creation, digital marketing, or related fields.
  • Familiarity with visual design principles, basic graphic design tools such as Canva
  • Passion for social media is a plus
  • Comfortable using key social media platforms like Instagram, LinkedIn, and Facebook
  • Strong writing, communication, and collaboration skills are essential to effectively work with the marketing team and contribute to various projects.

MUST be available to work onsite at our Minitab office in Chicago, IL.

Remote work is not an option for this position.

Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

Pursuant to the Illinois pay transparency laws, Minitab is posting the hourly compensation for this position. At the time of posting, the Illinois payrate is $18.00/hr.

This position is ineligible for visa sponsorship.

To be considered for this role, you must be legally authorized to work in the United States and not require sponsorship for employment now or in the future.

title: Marketing Intern
description_html:
ThinkMarkets is a global online brokerage firm, specializing in multi-asset trading solutions to thousands of clients around the world. With our flagship ThinkTrader platform, we allow our clients to trade the world 24 hours a day. Our mission is to build an AI-first trading platform by providing access to global markets, advanced trading features and thousands of products across multiple geographies so our clients can trade the world. We use the latest technologies to give traders seamless access to our proprietary trading platforms.

We are seeking a Marketing Intern to join our global team in Chicago. This is a role for an aspiring marketer who believes that the future of marketing is AI-augmented. You'll support retail trader acquisition, learn performance marketing, and work with AI tools to scale output.

This is an entry-level role suited to a motivated, data-curious person with an interest in marketing technology and AI-driven creative.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support acquisition campaigns across paid media, SEM, and mobile app marketing.
  • Help manage and analyse the marketing funnel, tracking conversion rates, engagement, and key metrics such as CAC and LTV.
  • Learn and apply tools within the Google Marketing Platform (Google Ads, GA4).
  • Contribute to mobile app growth initiatives, including ASO and app install campaigns.
  • Use AI tools and automation to support creative production, reporting, and campaign optimisation.
  • Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Operations on messaging, experimentation, and go-to-market initiatives.
  • Assist with budget tracking, reporting, and performance dashboards.

Requirements:

  • Recently completed (or currently completing) a degree in Marketing, Business, Computer Science, or a quantitative field.
  • Strong interest in fintech, trading platforms, or financial markets (no prior trading experience required).
  • An "AI-first" mindset with curiosity around automation, growth tools, and modern marketing workflows.
  • Comfortable working with data, spreadsheets, and basic reporting.

Internship Details

  • Duration: 6 months (extendable based on performance)
  • Type: Full-time, stipend-based
  • Location: Chicago office
  • Potential: High-performing interns may be considered for full-time roles

title: Design Student Intern - High School
description_html:
If you think your skills, experience and aspirations make you a good match for this position, we encourage you to apply.

About The Role

This internship will work under the supervision of a Licensed Architect and project leadership, providing students with an opportunity to be a member of our multi-disciplinary team.

Here's What You'll Do

  • Participate in and observe the design process.
  • Participate in the review of construction phase documentation.
  • Attend project, team and office meetings.
  • Explore various software platforms such as Revit, the Adobe Creative Suite, Rhino, Sketch-up, and Grasshopper.
  • Assist with building physical building models.
  • Assist with product research.
  • Attend in-house project team meetings.
  • May perform other duties as required.

Here's What You'll Need

  • Must be a recent High School graduate with a strong interest in pursuing a degree in the AEC industry.
  • Strong communication, teamwork, presentation, and organizational skills are preferred.

The salary range for this position to be filled in the Chicago office is $17.95 to $20.25 per hour. This salary range is the range we believe is the anticipated range of possible base compensation at the timing of the posting. We may ultimately pay more or less than the anticipated salary range for the position. Employees may be eligible for discretionary bonuses. We offer a full benefit package including medical, dental and vision coverage and flexible spending account options and voluntary insurances. We have paid time off, flex-time schedules, remote work options and a 401k plan and employee perk programs. For a general overview of our benefits, please visit our careers page at https://www.cannondesign.com/careers/benefits. Actual compensation may vary from posting based on geographic location, work experience, education and/or skill level.

About Our Firm

CannonDesign is a design practice where strategy, experience, architecture, engineering, and social impact converge. We don’t just dream up solutions — we create and bring them to life in ways to solve some of the biggest challenges facing our clients and the society. At the heart of everything we do is Living-Centered Design, a bold commitment to using our talents to not just improve the world but to truly reshape it for the better. It’s more than a philosophy — it’s who we are, and it drives us to make a meaningful, lasting difference every day.

About Working Here

  • We are relentless in our pursuit of client adoration (not simply satisfaction). Consistent delivery of the best service is what we are about.
  • We are committed to ensuring our practice provides equal opportunities for all employees, as we strive to connect with communities around us and focus on the future of design. We support equity, diversity, and inclusion efforts that benefit all employees through the leadership of our DEI Council, our Employee Resource Groups which are open to everyone, and other community initiatives.
  • We’re about communication and transparency here. If you want to talk to someone about an idea you have, or a challenge that needs addressing, we’re ready for you.

Please note that candidates can only apply to our positions on our company Careers site. It's not uncommon for scammers to create positions that look legitimate on other sites; never enter your information or apply for CannonDesign positions on any platform. Should an issue arise that you feel we should be aware of, please contact us. Please provide your resume and portfolio when applying.

As a condition of employment, all employees are expected to complete mandatory training, including compliance training, within required timeframes and adhere to our internal policies and our Code of Conduct.

CannonDesign is an Equal Opportunity Employer. CannonDesign is committed to maintaining a work environment that is free from any and all forms of unlawful discrimination and harassment. It is therefore the firm’s policy to prohibit discrimination and harassment against any applicant, CannonDesign employee, vendor, contractor, or client on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, pregnancy, veteran status, genetic information, citizenship status, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law. It is also CannonDesign’s policy to prohibit any and all forms of retaliation against any individual who has complained of harassing or discriminatory conduct, or participated in a firm or agency investigation into such complaints.

As a condition of employment, all employees are expected to complete mandatory training, including compliance training, within required timeframes and adhere to our internal policies and our Code of Conduct.

CannonDesign is an Equal Opportunity Employer. CannonDesign is committed to maintaining a work environment that is free from any and all forms of unlawful discrimination and harassment. It is therefore the firm’s policy to prohibit discrimination and harassment against any applicant, CannonDesign employee, vendor, contractor, or client on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, pregnancy, veteran status, genetic information, citizenship status, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law. It is also CannonDesign’s policy to prohibit any and all forms of retaliation against any individual who has complained of harassing or discriminatory conduct, or participated in a firm or agency investigation into such complaints.

title: Innovation Marketing Internship
description_html:
Position Title: Global Snacking - Innovation Marketing Intern

Position Type: Temporary - Full-Time

Grade: Grade 01

Requisition ID: 41499

Innovation Marketing Internship.

Oakbrook, IL

In every role, McCainers are ambitious, curious, and passionate about creating exceptional work experiences - together. With a customer-first mindset, we make doing business with McCain easy.

About The Role.

We are seeking an Innovation Marketing Intern to support our Global Snacking Innovation Marketing team for a 12-week internship beginning in May 2026. This role is designed for a curious, analytical, and future-focused marketer who is passionate about innovation, food trends, and retail strategy.

In this position, you will build critical strategic thinking skills required for a career in innovation marketing, brand management or consumer insights. You will gain hands-on exposure to how consumer and operator insights translate into innovation strategies, pipeline decisions, and retail-ready solutions that come to life in the marketplace.

A core focus of this role will be helping the team identify innovation white space opportunities in the retail channel across the globe by turning data into insights and insights into “jobs to be done”—ultimately helping McCain differentiate, grow, and win with consumers and customers.

What You’ll Be Doing

Innovation & Strategy Development

  • Support the team in diving into McCain’s Global Snacking Innovation pipeline architecture, with a focus on Tastiez/Pickers platform
  • Explore and evaluate Better-for-You (BFY) trends, Ethnic flavors and formats, and pack-size innovation to unlock new availability and retail opportunities
  • Translate consumer, shopper, and operator data into actionable insights and “jobs to be done” that inform future innovation and renovation
  • Identify white space opportunities that strengthen McCain’s retail differentiation

Retail Insights

  • Develop a strong understanding of how insights drive strategy across the Retail channels
  • Analyze how innovation shows up at shelf and on menu—linking consumer needs to retail execution
  • Support projects that examine how to build the Tastiez/Pickers portfolio within the category

Data, Analytics & Insight Storytelling

  • Build data analytics skills using external data sources such as NPD and IRI
  • Mine insights from McCain’s Demand Landscape and Sub-Spaces to inform innovation priorities
  • Participate in the creation and presentation of Key Insights and Strategic Recommendations for cross-functional partners

Learning & Collaboration

  • Own real-world initiatives that evolve into strategies executed in the marketplace
  • Gain exposure to brand marketing, communication development, and new product innovation
  • Receive 1:1 coaching and mentorship from Innovation team members and cross-functional partners

What You’ll Need To Be Successful.

  • Currently working toward a Bachelor’s degree in Marketing or Business Strategy
  • Strong curiosity—always asking why consumers behave the way they do and what’s next
  • Interest in food, food culture, and emerging food trends
  • Passion for innovation, retail strategy, and future-forward thinking
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills
  • A self-starter who is comfortable working independently and collaboratively
  • Analytical mindset with a love for digging into data and uncovering insights
  • Ability to commute to the Oakbrook Terrace, IL office and work a hybrid schedule (3 days/week onsite)

About The Team.

This intern will join the McCain Global Snacking Innovation team, comprised of 65 other marketing Innovation professionals in the US and Canada who serve as the lead on disruptive innovation planning and global pipeline architecture.

About McCain.

Click Here to learn more about McCain and how we provide you with opportunities to make an impact that matters.

Leadership principles.

At McCain, our leadership principles guide how we engage with customers, collaborate as a team, and achieve success. We focus on understanding customer needs, driving innovation, empowering people, and taking ownership to clear obstacles and deliver results.

The McCain Experience.

We are McCain. This statement is a testament to our collective strength and our individual value. Your contributions play a vital role in our success. Our winning culture is rooted in authenticity and trust, empowering us to bring out the best in one another. Here, you’ll find opportunities to learn, grow, and thrive. Join us and experience why we’re better together.

Compensation Package: $23.00 - $26.00 USD hourly wage equivalent, paid as a non-exempt salary.

The above reflects the target compensation range for the position at the time of posting. Hiring compensation will be determined based on experience, skill set, education and training, and other organizational needs. This posting is for an existing vacancy.

Benefits: At McCain, we’re on a mission to create a winning culture that puts employee safety and well-being at the heart of what we do, every day. We understand and appreciate that each person’s needs are unique and ensure our benefits & well-being programs reflect that. Due to the duration of this role, you will not be eligible for traditional benefits through our programs. McCain's early careers programming offers a multitude of learning experiences aimed at advancing your career in the future.

Your well-being matters to us, and we’re here to provide you with the necessary resources to support you in being your best self at work — and at home.

McCain Foods is an equal opportunity employer. As a global family-owned company, we strive to be the employer of choice in the diverse communities around the world in which we live and work. We recognize that inclusion drives our creativity, resilience, and success and makes our business stronger. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected characteristic under applicable law.

McCain is an accessible employer. If you require an accommodation throughout the recruitment process (including alternate formats of materials or accessible meeting rooms), please let us know and we will work with you to find appropriate solutions.

Your privacy is important to us. By submitting personal data or information to us, you agree this will be handled in accordance with McCain’s Global Privacy Policy and Global Employee Privacy Policy, as applicable. McCain leverages AI in the hiring process, though all final decisions are made by humans. You can understand our approach to AI and how your personal information is being handled here.

title: Architecture - Student Intern (Summer 2026)
description_html:
HOK is a collective of future-forward thinkers and designers who are driven to face the critical challenges of our time. We are dedicated to improving people's lives, serving our clients and healing the planet. Together, we cultivate a culture of design excellence at the confluence of art and science, blending the power of creative expression with a clear sense of purpose.

Our Chicago studio is currently seeking an Architecture intern to join our team this summer. This internship offers an exciting opportunity for students pursuing a degree in architecture to gain practical, hands-on experience. You'll work on real-world projects, learn from and collaborate with talented professionals, and develop skills in a dynamic studio environment.

Application must include resume and portfolio (or portfolio link).

Responsibilities

  • Develops solutions to technical and design problems following established standards.
  • Incorporates Integrated Sustainable Design solutions into projects.
  • Prepares 2D and 3D presentation/design drawings.
  • Builds models or prepares project boards for client/project team review.
  • Takes personal responsibility for fostering a green workplace through sustainable work practices.
  • Fosters a commitment to external and internal client service.

Education & Experience

  • Currently enrolled in an architecture or related degree program.

Skills & Competencies

  • Proficiency in 3D modeling software including Rhino, Grasshopper and Enscape.
  • Ability to build physical models.
  • Basic knowledge in Revit is preferred.
  • Proficiency in Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign or Affinity software is preferred.
  • Basic knowledge about sustainability, integrated design and LEED guidelines.
  • Ability to effectively meet deadlines.
  • Ability to communicate verbally and in writing.
  • Ability to work in a team environment.

Many of our roles require the use of specific software. Please refer to the Required and Preferred Software & Skills for details.

Wherever you are in your career, HOK provides opportunities to learn, develop and utilize your skills. Continued learning is promoted through financial support and study prep for licensure exams, CEU credit opportunities, mentorship programs, and paid professional memberships. To learn more about designing your career with HOK, explore our current job openings.

HOK is an EOE, including disability/vets.

Compensation is based on experience, qualifications, education and location. The reasonably expected hourly rate for this position is $25.00

title: Integrated Marketing Internship
description_html:
About The Internship

Lawson Products and Kent Automotive are looking for a high-energy, creative, and curious Integrated Marketing Intern who’s excited to see how marketing comes together across multiple channels.

If you’re interested in how campaigns move from idea to execution — across email, web, sales, and digital channels — and you enjoy learning how teams collaborate to deliver a consistent message, this internship is for you.

You’ll work closely with our marketing team and gain hands-on experience supporting integrated campaigns for two established B2B brands.

What You’ll Do

As an Integrated Marketing Intern, you’ll support the planning, execution, and analysis of marketing initiatives across channels, including:

  • Assisting with integrated marketing campaigns across email, digital media, web, social, and sales enablement channels
  • Supporting campaign execution, timelines, and coordination across teams
  • Helping review and QA marketing assets to ensure brand consistency and accuracy
  • Assisting with content updates for web, email, and promotional materials
  • Supporting basic performance reporting and campaign analysis
  • Conducting competitive and industry research to inform campaign ideas
  • Jumping into special projects that support marketing strategy and execution

Who We’re Looking For

We’re looking for someone who brings energy, curiosity, and a willingness to learn.

You’ll Be a Great Fit If You Are

  • Currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field
  • High-energy and eager to learn, with an interest in how marketing works across channels
  • Curious about how campaigns are planned, launched, and measured
  • Organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple projects
  • A strong communicator who enjoys working with cross-functional teams
  • Comfortable learning new tools and platforms
  • Proactive and not afraid to ask questions

Nice To Have (But Not Required)

  • Experience with email marketing, content creation, or campaign management (class projects count!)
  • Familiarity with digital marketing, social media, or basic analytics
  • Previous internship, campus organization, or marketing-related experience

What You’ll Gain

  • Hands-on experience working on real integrated marketing campaigns
  • Exposure to how marketing aligns with field sales, eCommerce, and digital teams
  • Insight into B2B marketing strategy and execution
  • Opportunities to contribute ideas and see them implemented
  • Mentorship from experienced marketing professionals
  • Resume-building experience with nationally recognized brands

The hourly rate for this role is $19.50.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Lawson Products and Kent Automotive are equal opportunity employers and welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

title: Marketing Internship
description_html:
Position Summary

Nautilus Solar Energy® is a leading owner-operator of solar projects focused on large industrial, and community solar markets located throughout the United States. A pioneer in solar energy since 2006, Nautilus is committed to creating a clean, sustainable future by offering an equitable and affordable renewable energy choice. Through its Community Solar initiative, Nautilus is making solar energy available to a broader marketplace, including low to middle income (LMI) households and unrated businesses that wish to reduce their carbon footprint and utility bills.

Nautilus is wholly-owned by Power Sustainable, a global multi-platform alternative asset manager with a long-term investment approach focused on sustainable strategies. Nautilus owns solar projects throughout their entire lifecycle enabling Nautilus, along with entrusted local partners, to fulfill its promise of creating a better world by powering communities with clean energy for the long term.

Nautilus is committed to creating a strong culture of diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) by acknowledging that our differences make us stronger, together we are powerful, and we lead with intention.

About The Internship Program

The Nautilus Solar Internship Program will offer undergraduate students a paid, 10-12-week, hands-on experience assisting in the development, acquisition, and operation of distributed solar assets, while learning more about the solar and renewable energy industries. As a Nautilus intern you will gain experience in community solar, solar development, distributed generation and project financing. These opportunities offer a unique blend of analytical, financial and technology experience working alongside experienced industry veterans. This position will be highly interactive with customers, partners, and Nautilus personnel. The Marketing Intern will work closely with the Marketing team to maximize community solar subscriber operations, assist with marketing and customer service needs, and learn about the overall process

of acquiring and managing subscribers on community solar projects from start to finish. This position will consist of a variety of different tasks, all of which will be necessary for the growth and development of Nautilus Community Solar. The internship will be hybrid for 2 days in our Chicago office.

Requirements

Responsibilities:

  • Join the marketing and communications department with the opportunity to work on high-visibility initiatives such as integrated marketing campaigns that directly impact pipeline and product adoption across multiple regions and audiences
  • Help craft how Nautilus presents itself in the market and ensure our story resonates throughout the funnel—from initial touch to finish. This may involve brainstorming, developing, and refining messaging and creative assets—including ad copy, landing pages, nurture workflows
  • Build and optimize paid media plans across platforms such as Google, Meta, Reddit, and niche D2C channels
  • Monitor ad performance, uncover insights, and recommend experiments to improve conversion and lower acquisition costs
  • Assist team with ad hoc needs across marketing and communications to include event planning, social media posting, copywriting, web maintenance, and more

Requirements:

  • Enrolled as a student or recent graduate
  • Available 36 hours/week, for 10-12 weeks
  • Studies in Business, Marketing, Communications, Environmental Studies or relevant to energy or related sectors preferred but not required
  • Interest in solar and renewable energy industry
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Microsoft Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint software proficiency
  • Comfort working in a remote environment
  • Interest in growth marketing, digital campaigns, funnel analytics, or CRM/data workflows
  • Curiosity, willingness to learn, and a proactive, experimentation-driven mindset
  • Familiarity with Hubspot, paid media platforms, marketing automation tools, or AI workflows a bonus!
  • Interns will need to provide their own laptop
  • Unrestricted authorization to work in the US

title: Marketing Intern
description_html:
About Coinflow

Coinflow is the next-generation payment service provider revolutionizing global financial infrastructure with stablecoins, AI-driven fraud prevention, and instant settlement. Coinflow enables businesses to grow faster with instant settlement, fraud & chargeback indemnity, global pay-ins, multi-currency FX, and unified payouts. Founded in 2023, the company serves marketplaces, fintechs, remittance providers, gaming platforms, and ecommerce merchants worldwide.

Since our seed round in 2024, we’ve achieved 23x revenue growth and scaled to multi-billion-dollar annual transaction volume. In response to this growth, Coinflow announced a $25M Series A in October 2025—led by Pantera Capital, CMT Digital, Coinbase Ventures, Jump Crypto, and Reciprocal Ventures—accelerating our mission to power the world’s fastest-moving businesses with innovative, reliable global payments.

Coinflow is proudly headquartered in Chicago, IL. Learn more at coinflow.cash.

Role Overview

Coinflow is looking for a part-time Summer Marketing Intern to work directly with our Head of Marketing across several marketing initiatives, including event marketing, content creation, social media, product marketing, and market research.

This role is ideal for a student interested in gaining hands-on experience in startup marketing, fintech, and crypto, while contributing meaningfully to real marketing campaigns and initiatives.

The internship will run 12 weeks during Summer 2026 at approximately 20 hours per week.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support the planning and execution of marketing events from concept through post-event reporting
  • Assist with content marketing initiatives, including blog posts, newsletters, videos, etc.
  • Conduct market research and competitive analysis within the fintech and crypto industries
  • Contribute to Coinflow’s social media strategy by helping plan, create, and schedule social content
  • Assist with product marketing efforts, including messaging, positioning, and marketing collateral
  • Track marketing performance and help prepare reports on campaign effectiveness
  • Collaborate closely with the Head of Marketing and cross-functionally on various strategic and operational marketing projects

Required Qualifications

  • Currently enrolled undergraduate student (or recent graduate) studying marketing, business, communications, or a related field
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines
  • Interest in startups, fintech, or crypto
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced and collaborative environment

Preferred Qualifications

  • Previous internship or project experience in marketing, startups, or technology
  • Experience with social media platforms such as LinkedIn and X
  • Familiarity with growth tools such as HubSpot, Loops, Attio, Canva, or similar platforms
  • Interest in event marketing or community building
  • Curiosity about fintech, crypto infrastructure, or payments

Join the team rewriting how money moves worldwide—and become a driving force in the $194 trillion cross-border payments market.

Since our seed round in 2024, we’ve achieved 23x revenue growth and scaled to multi-billion-dollar annual transaction volume. In response to this growth, Coinflow announced a $25M Series A in October 2025—led by Pantera Capital, CMT Digital, Coinbase Ventures, Jump Crypto, and Reciprocal Ventures—accelerating our mission to power the world’s fastest-moving businesses with innovative, reliable global payments.

Coinflow is proudly headquartered in Chicago, IL. Learn more at coinflow.cash.

Role Overview

Coinflow is looking for a part-time Summer Marketing Intern to work directly with our Head of Marketing across several marketing initiatives, including event marketing, content creation, social media, product marketing, and market research.

This role is ideal for a student interested in gaining hands-on experience in startup marketing, fintech, and crypto, while contributing meaningfully to real marketing campaigns and initiatives.

The internship will run 12 weeks during Summer 2026 at approximately 20 hours per week.

Key Responsibilities


Support the planning and execution of marketing events from concept through post-event reporting
Assist with content marketing initiatives, including blog posts, newsletters, videos, etc.
Conduct market research and competitive analysis within the fintech and crypto industries
Contribute to Coinflow’s social media strategy by helping plan, create, and schedule social content
Assist with product marketing efforts, including messaging, positioning, and marketing collateral
Track marketing performance and help prepare reports on campaign effectiveness
Collaborate closely with the Head of Marketing and cross-functionally on various strategic and operational marketing projects


Required Qualifications


Currently enrolled undergraduate student (or recent graduate) studying marketing, business, communications, or a related field
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Highly organized with strong attention to detail
Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines
Interest in startups, fintech, or crypto
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and collaborative environment


Preferred Qualifications


Previous internship or project experience in marketing, startups, or technology
Experience with social media platforms such as LinkedIn and X
Familiarity with growth tools such as HubSpot, Loops, Attio, Canva, or similar platforms
Interest in event marketing or community building
Curiosity about fintech, crypto infrastructure, or payments


Join the team rewriting how money moves worldwide—and become a driving force in the $194 trillion cross-border payments market.


Show more

title: Marketing Internship
description_html:
Position Title: NA Commercial - Marketing Internship - NA Foodservice - Summer 2026

Position Type: Temporary - Full-Time

Grade: Grade 01

Requisition ID: 41160

Marketing Internship – Food Service Portfolio Strategy.

Oakbrook, IL

In every role, McCainers are ambitious, curious, and passionate about creating exceptional work experiences - together. With a customer-first mindset, we make doing business with McCain easy.

About The Role.

This internship presents an exceptional opportunity to join McCain Foods, the global leader in potato food service, and work directly with the most extensive Foodservice and Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) portfolio in the industry. Our Marketing team is seeking a motivated intern to collaborate with us for a 12-week placement starting in May 2026.

As an intern, you will contribute strategically to our evolving food service portfolio, engaging in high-impact projects that will drive meaningful commercial transformation and help shape the future direction of our brands.

This position is based at our Oakbrook, IL office, with an expectation of working on-site three days per week as part of our hybrid work model.

What You’ll Be Doing

  • Engage in a comprehensive analysis of our business and competitive landscape, benchmarking McCain’s portfolio against leading industry competitors.
  • Collaborate with team members to reset portfolio architecture, segmenting operator needs and refining product assortment and tiering.
  • Support the execution of portfolio consolidation strategies, including SKU and brand consolidation, as well as evaluation of distributor and co-branding opportunities.
  • Assist in developing commercial tools and materials to present and sell new strategies to our sales teams.
  • Contribute to the launch of new product SKUs based on identified market gaps and growth opportunities.
  • Utilize marketing insights and data-driven analysis to deliver actionable recommendations.
  • Participate in the creation of exceptional execution plans and learn the commercial sales process.

What You’ll Need To Be Successful.

  • Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Marketing (or a related field).
  • Demonstrated interest in food and emerging food trends.
  • Strong analytical abilities and a talent for translating data insights into business actions.
  • Self-starter with the ability to work independently, as well as collaboratively within teams.
  • Passion for diving deep into data and uncovering meaningful insights.
  • Ability to commute to the Oakbrook, IL office for a hybrid schedule (three days per week in-office).

About The Team.

This intern will join the McCain North American Marketing Foodservice and QSR (Quick Service Restaurant) team. The team is comprised of 8 other Brand Marketing professionals in the US and Canada who serve as Strategic Marketing Partners to the organization, executing portfolio and marketing strategies to deliver key McCain brand portfolios to drive our strategic ambitions.

About McCain.

Click Here to learn more about McCain and how we provide you with opportunities to make an impact that matters.

Leadership principles.

At McCain, our leadership principles guide how we engage with customers, collaborate as a team, and achieve success. We focus on understanding customer needs, driving innovation, empowering people, and taking ownership to clear obstacles and deliver results.

The McCain Experience.

We are McCain. This statement is a testament to our collective strength and our individual value. Your contributions play a vital role in our success. Our winning culture is rooted in authenticity and trust, empowering us to bring out the best in one another. Here, you’ll find opportunities to learn, grow, and thrive. Join us and experience why we’re better together.

Compensation Package: $23.00 - 26.00 USD hourly wage equivalent, paid as a non-exempt salary.

The above reflects the target compensation range for the position at the time of posting. Hiring compensation will be determined based on experience, skill set, education and training, and other organizational needs. This posting is for an existing vacancy.

Benefits: At McCain, we’re on a mission to create a winning culture that puts employee safety and well-being at the heart of what we do, every day. We understand and appreciate that each person’s needs are unique and ensure our benefits & well-being programs reflect that. Due to the duration of this role, you will not be eligible for traditional benefits through our programs. McCain's early careers programming offers a multitude of learning experiences aimed at advancing your career in the future.

Your well-being matters to us, and we’re here to provide you with the necessary resources to support you in being your best self at work — and at home.

McCain Foods is an equal opportunity employer. As a global family-owned company, we strive to be the employer of choice in the diverse communities around the world in which we live and work. We recognize that inclusion drives our creativity, resilience, and success and makes our business stronger. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected characteristic under applicable law.

McCain is an accessible employer. If you require an accommodation throughout the recruitment process (including alternate formats of materials or accessible meeting rooms), please let us know and we will work with you to find appropriate solutions.

Your privacy is important to us. By submitting personal data or information to us, you agree this will be handled in accordance with McCain’s Global Privacy Policy and Global Employee Privacy Policy, as applicable. McCain leverages AI in the hiring process, though all final decisions are made by humans. You can understand our approach to AI and how your personal information is being handled here.

title: Marketing Intern
description_html:
Impassioned…Emboldened…Infinitely Curious: The Bradford Group family of brands is a world leader in new product development. We have been selling joy for over 50 years! Our global organization consists of various business divisions ranging from jewelry, apparel, collectible coins and so much more. We concept, create, design and market our creative products directly to our customers, and leverage licenses with the biggest names in entertainment and sports to bring unique and curated products to our customers across the globe.

We are seeking outstanding Marketing Interns to assist various business units within The Bradford Group with the execution of direct marketing advertising campaigns in this 10-week summer experience. (6/16-8/20) This position requires strong communication skills, experience with Microsoft Excel and emerging AI tools, and the ability to prioritize assignments and work in a fast-paced environment. Throughout your internship you will assist marketing managers in conducting quantitative analysis on specific customer segments, track revenue results and projections, advise product development teams on the wants/needs of customer segments, and organize direct mail advertising campaigns. This is a unique opportunity to gain marketing experience in a hands-on role at a world leader in the gifts and collectibles industry. Located just outside of Chicago in Niles, IL, this full time internship (40 hour/week) is a paid position.

How You Will Contribute

  • Analyze data and provide key customer insights to drive decisions on new product and advertising campaigns.
  • Perform customer segmentation and provide recommendations to marketing team.
  • Analyze effectiveness of marketing campaigns.
  • Leverage emerging AI tools and automation technologies to streamline workflows, enhance marketing operations, and identify opportunities for process improvement and efficiency.

What You Will Bring And Skills That Excite Us

  • Preference is given to Junior and Senior candidates.
  • Pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related field.
  • Must possess excellent organizational and communication skills (verbal & written).
  • Familiarity with emerging AI based technologies.
  • Strong computer skills including Microsoft Excel & Word.
  • Experience with SPSS, Access or other databases a plus.
  • Experience with direct marketing is a plus.
  • Quantitative, analytical, and detail-oriented mindset, with the ability to work on multiple projects simultaneously.

What You Can Expect

  • Paid internship
  • Hybrid work environment-M,F are remote and T-Thurs are in-office
  • Network and build relationships with peers
  • Intern events, lunches, outings and more
  • Professional development and training sessions
  • Creative work environment
  • On site fitness center

Bradford At a Glance

  • Our scale: We have over 250 employees based out of our global headquarters right outside of Chicago, we have marketing operations in over 10 countries worldwide, and have over 32 million customers served.
  • Our values: Honesty. Innovation. Respect. Teamwork. Giving back. These are just some of the core values that drive our team members. Our team strives to build a culture of continuous feedback where anyone can share and create our “next big idea,” among diverse and inclusive teams.

Compensation

 $20.00 per hour

The Bradford Exchange is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Marketing Internship | First-Generation College Students
description_html:
Job Description

This Marketing Internship program is designed and available for first-generation college students.

As a Marketing Intern, you will do more than contribute. You will take ownership. From day one, you will help shape strategies that drive business growth, elevate our brand, and strengthen how we connect with the clients and prospects who matter most. You will be immersed in real marketing work within a fast-paced, collaborative environment where your thinking, creativity, and execution can make a measurable impact.

Throughout the summer, you will contribute across high-impact initiatives and lead a dedicated project designed to deliver meaningful, lasting value to the organization. This is not a hypothetical assignment. It is work that will continue to create impact beyond your internship and serve as a tangible example of your leadership and results.

You will gain exposure to a broad range of marketing focus areas, including Event Marketing, Brand and Talent Brand, Revenue Marketing including lead and demand generation, Product Marketing, PR, and Communications. Along the way, you will collaborate with cross-functional partners, learn how modern marketing drives growth at scale, and build practical skills that accelerate your career.

This internship runs from June 3rd through August 7th 2026.

Interns are expected to work onsite at our Chicago office Monday through Friday.

What will you do:

  • Learn to undertake research and analysis to help us understand opportunity areas against our competition.
  • Provide insights and feedback to the marketing teams to support our growth as well as enabling us to strengthen and deepen the insights we provide across the organization.
  • Support the launch of multichannel campaigns and journeys, analyzing the performance of these campaigns and journeys to build a foundation of data acumen.
  • Assess the awareness of our brand through research to understand quarter over quarter improvements.
  • Drive event strategies that are aligned to our objectives and help execute a series of events.
  • Partner with functions across the marketing and communications team to understand how cross-function teams work together to launch work and optimize how they work together to continually improve our work.

What you will gain:

  • New skills and build on the strengths you bring to the company.
  • Direct exposure to a technology company that provides enterprise AI platforms and services, learn about the business, and experience working in a global/transnational team.
  • Hands-on experience leveraging AI to enhance marketing execution and productivity. You will learn how modern teams use AI to work smarter, move faster, and drive measurable impact.
  • Access to a support team (People Manager, Early Careers Marketing Buddy, Mentors, Early Careers Advisor) to provide ongoing career support and guidance throughout the program.
  • Opportunities for training as well as frequent coaching and mentoring from dedicated members of the marketing team.
  • Access to a series of leadership presentations and exposure to marketing executives and others across our company.
  • Networking and professional development opportunities via lunch and learns, fireside chats, intern social events, and a Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR)/Social Impact initiative.
  • A concrete view into marketing and the opportunity to explore all functions and potential roles that exist within the Publicis Sapient Marketing Organization.
  • At the end of the program, you will share your impact and learnings through a final presentation and be supported in the development and delivery by your supervisor.

Qualifications

Qualifications

  • Identify as a First-Generation College Student
    • A first-generation college student is a person whose parents or guardians have not earned a four-year bachelor’s degree.
  • Rising college Senior who is currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in marketing, with a strong academic record and demonstrated interest in brand building, growth strategy, and modern marketing practices. Alternatively, students pursuing a bachelor’s degree in business administration, communications, public relations, or economics must have a declared minor in marketing and a clear passion for applying marketing strategy to drive business impact.
  • Strong project management and operational aptitude with the ability to manage multiple projects and initiatives.
  • Self-starter who is results-oriented, resourceful, analytical, innovative, intellectually curious and takes initiative and risks.
  • Excellent written and verbal skills.
  • Demonstrate strong ability to embrace and adapt to change, take initiative, and display curiosity, learn quickly in a fast-paced environment.
  • Experience and proficiency in Microsoft Office tools/programs.
  • Previous work experience, clubs, volunteerism, or internships is a plus.
Nice to Have:

  • Experience in project management/event management is a plus.
  • Exposure to or firsthand experience using AI tools to enhance execution and productivity (e.g., applying AI within Excel, project management platforms, research, content development, copy, or workflow optimization).

Eligibility

  • Undergraduate with completion of Junior Year
  • Available for a 10-week internship from June 3rd to August 7th 2026 and able to come into our Chicago office daily.
  • Available to work 40 hours per week.

Additional Information

Compensation: $31.00 per hour

As part of our dedication to an inclusive and diverse workforce, Publicis Sapient is committed to Equal Employment Opportunity without regard for race, color, national origin, ethnicity, gender, protected veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or religion. We are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures.

If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at hiring@publicis.sapient.com

Company Description

Publicis Sapient is a digital transformation partner helping established organizations get to their future, digitally enabled state, both in the way they work and the way they serve their customers. We help unlock value through a start-up mindset and modern methods, fusing strategy, consulting, and customer experience with agile engineering and problem-solving creativity. United by our core values and our purpose of helping people thrive in the brave pursuit of next, our 20,000+ people in 53 offices around the world combine experience across technology, data sciences, consulting, and customer obsession to accelerate our clients’ businesses through designing the products and services their customers truly value.
