title: Intern, RFIC Design Validation and Characterization - Summer 2026
description_html:
pSemi Corporation is a Murata company driving semiconductor integration. pSemi builds on Peregrine Semiconductor’s 30-year legacy of technology advancements and strong IP portfolio but with a new mission—to enhance Murata’s world-class capabilities with high-performance semiconductors. With a strong foundation in RF integration, pSemi’s product portfolio now spans power management, connected sensors, optical transceivers, antenna tuning and RF frontends. These intelligent and efficient semiconductors enable advanced modules for smartphones, base stations, personal computers, electric vehicles, data centers, IoT devices and healthcare. From headquarters in San Diego and offices around the world, pSemi’s team explores new ways to make electronics for the connected world smaller, thinner, faster and better.

Job Summary

The RFIC Intern will assist engineers in designing, developing, and characterizing pSemi’s product development efforts for mobile wireless RF front end semiconductor products for cellular handsets. Product lines include high performance RF switches, PA controllers, power amplifiers, low noise amplifiers, digital tunable capacitors and IC filters. The candidate will support products throughout the entire product life cycle from product definition through product release to end-of-life.

Roles & Responsibilities

This position has responsibility for:

  • Design Validation: Performing lab measurements and create validation report with comparisons vs design expectations.
  • Test and Measurement: Hands on use of RF measurement equipment to validate performance of CMOS RF ICs.
  • Product Characterization: Measure, analyze and document data of adherence to product requirements.

Minimum Qualifications (Experience And Skills)

  • Knowledge of RF measurements – NF, IP3, IP2, IMD, ACLR, EVM, S-parameters, matching using smith charts, time domain measurements etc.
  • Knowledge of analog and RF IC Design
  • Familiarity with EM concepts and RF layout techniques (board or interposer)
  • Software tools: Matlab, JMP, Python, Datapower, National Instruments LabView, Cadence, ADS

Preferred Qualifications

  • RF/analog IC product characterization and RF measurement techniques with strong technical skills
  • Strength in documentation clarity and completeness
  • Team player with a strong sense of urgency to meet product requirements on schedule
  • Hands-on use of lab equipment to validate performance such as Spectrum Analyzers, Network analyzers, signal generators, oscilloscopes, DMM, SMU etc.

Education Requirements

  • Fulltime enrollment in accredited Bachelor’s degree program in Electrical Engineer (must be completing 3+ year) or MSEE candidate

Work Environment

This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office equipment.

Physical Demands

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk or hear. The employee frequently is required to stand; walk; use hands to finger, handle or feel; and reach with hands and arms. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus. This position requires the ability to occasionally lift office products and supplies, up to 20 pounds.

USD 25.63 - 48.60

title: Sustainable Design Intern
description_html:
Job Description

Ryan Companies has an exciting opportunity to join our team as a Sustainable Design Intern in our Chicago office!

This position requires a creative, innovative, collaborative and forward-thinking student enrolled in a design program focusing on Architecture or Interior Design. This person will be working under direct supervision of the Sustainability Team to gain experience in multiple areas of building analysis and ideation. The successful candidate will be willing to take specific direction on project and department activities and execute in a professional manner.

Roles And Responsibilities

  • Works under direct supervision of Sustainability Director, Sustainability Design Manager, and other design project team members to do the following tasks:
  • Prepare models and conduct preliminary sustainability analyses on design options using Climate Studio
  • Conduct site assessments and due diligence leveraging local codes, regulations, and climate conditions to recommend design solutions.
  • Prepare visually compelling reports communicating technical results and project stories to project teams and their clients.
  • Participating in design charrettes focused on delivering sustainability and design excellence.
  • Works with the project team to create diagrams and collect supporting documentation for external reporting and green certification programs such as LEED.
  • Solve problems and resolve technical issues.
  • Assist Sustainability Director with research and preparing educational content for internal trainings.
  • Participates in design meetings, consultant meetings and project team meetings.
  • Performs tasks with a high level of collaboration.
  • Organize and maintain project files, scan, copy, print, and transmit original working drawings of current projects.
  • Open to learning through listening and asking questions of team members and mentor(s).
  • Establishes and pursues annual goals; based on personal, professional and company growth in the industry.
  • Contributes to Ryan culture through participation in office activities, initiatives and learning programs.
  • Responsible for execution of Ryan’s yearly review and goal setting process based on personal and professional, development goals.
  • Actively participates in Ryan Companies’ and Ryan A+E’s 5-year vision/initiatives and 1-year business plans and goals.

Job Requirements

  • You must be enrolled in a design program focusing on Architecture, Interior Design, Landscape Architecture, or Civil Engineering.
  • Pursuing Professional education (Bachelor or Master’s degree or Technical degree programs) in Architecture or Interior Design.
  • Relevant professional or educational experience preferred.
  • Strong organizational skills.
  • Submission of design and research portfolio that demonstrates design and research process and solutions.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to collaborate with a diverse team.
  • Revit, Rhino and Adobe Creative Suite knowledge required.
  • Knowledge of various software programs including Climate Studio, Ladybug/Honeybee, OneClick LCA, Enscape, Bluebeam, and MS Office Suite preferred.
  • Willingness to learn various aspects of the design process including; Conceptual Design, Client Presentations, Building Codes, Documentation, Construction Administration, and Constructability.

Eligibility

Position requires verification of employment to work in the U.S.

Benefits

  • Competitive Salary
  • Medical, Dental and Vision Benefits
  • Retirement and Savings Benefits
  • Flexible Spending and Health Savings Accounts
  • Life Insurance
  • Short-Term and Long-Term Disability
  • Educational Assistance
  • Paid Time Off (PTO)
  • Employee Assistance and Wellness Programs
  • Parenting Benefits
  • Employee Discount Programs
  • Pet insurance
  • Ryan Foundation – charitable matching funds
  • Paid Time for Volunteer Events

Disclaimer: Eligibility may vary based on factors such as role, hours worked, employment status, length of service, location, and other considerations. Detailed information will be shared with eligible candidates during the hiring process, and the official terms and conditions will be outlined in each individual offer document.

Ryan Companies is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Non-Solicitation Notice To Recruitment Agencies

Ryan Companies kindly requests that recruitment agencies and third-party recruiters do not submit unsolicited resumes or candidate information to any Ryan Companies employee or office. Ryan Companies will not be responsible for any fees or expenses associated with unsolicited submissions. If recruitment services are required, we will reach out directly to agencies on our approved vendor list. We appreciate your understanding and cooperation.

title: Sustainability Intern (Summer 2026)
description_html:
Firm Overview

Do more than just build! At dbHMS, we are a collection of doers and thinkers. We combine our passion for engineering with our client’s vision to realize successful construction projects in our community and worldwide. Together with our clients, we are dedicated to leaving a legacy of stewardship and innovation through our impact on the built environment. Our staff of talented individuals and unique characters is united in their dedication to deliver high-performance, efficient, and sustainable buildings worldwide. 

Our DataBased+ Studio team in our Chicago office s seeking a Sustainability Intern. This role will support the engineering of systems for a variety of projects, creating designs for all kinds of spaces. 

Our dynamic work environment provides the perfect opportunity to advance your career while developing and fine-tuning your skills along with offering a supportive office culture, diverse workforce, flexible work schedules, and regular social events. At dbHMS, you will find everything you need for an exciting, challenging, and rewarding career.

dbHMS is committed to a diverse workforce and to providing a safe, accessible workplace. Most duties will be performed in an office environment. dbHMS currently operates with both hybrid and remote-only work policies. The remainder of the duties will be performed in a combination of office environment and construction/field environment which will involve trips to projects sites.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit, and frequently required to walk. The work typically requires the use of hands to type, handle or feel; reach with hands and arms; and talk or hear. The employee is regularly required to stand and perform work in the field. The typical week consists of five, eight-hour shifts.

Position Overview

In the DataBased+ Studio within dbHMS, Sustainability Interns ask and answer questions that lead to design decisions that improve human-centered outcomes, minimize cost, and maximize resource efficiency of buildings, campuses, neighborhoods, or regions.

Essential Job Functions

  • Analyze sustainability needs and identify opportunities for implementation.
  • Perform calculations, drawing reviews, and analyses related to sustainable certifications.
  • Using industry standard software tools to analyze the resource use of building systems and accurately predict their impact on human health and environmental sustainability.
  • Ability to read and understand construction documents, floor plans, specifications, and details.


Requirements

  • Minimum Education: Enrolled in a 4-year Engineering, Architecture, or Environmental Studies program with less than one year of experience.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Programs.


Additional Preferred Qualifications

  • Familiarity or experience with sustainable rating systems including but not limited to LEED, Enterprise Green Communities, Living Building Challenge, PHIUS, WELL, Fitwel.
  • Experience using one of the following building performance analysis software:
    • OneClick LCA, Athena, EC3, Tally, or other LCA software
    • LightStanza, ClimateStudio, or similar daylighting software
    • Open Studio/Energy Plus
    • Grasshopper plug-in and associated programs
    • REVIT, BIM360, or similar building information modeling software
Personal Qualities And Other Skills/Abilities

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Motivated self-starter and fast learner.
  • Strong time-management skills and experience managing multiple priorities at once.
  • Strong critical thinking skills.
  • Ability to collaborate in a team environment.
  • Passionate about human-centered outcomes.
  • Well-developed interpersonal skills, maturity, credibility, confidence, and integrity.

title: Civil Engineer - Drainage
description_html:
If you enjoy hands‑on design work, solving engineering challenges, and learning from experienced mentors, this role gives you the foundation to grow quickly in the drainage discipline.

At CBBEL, Our Values Guide Everything We Do

Integrity – We follow through on our promises.

Excellence – We’re always learning, growing, and raising the bar.

Fellowship – We support each other like a true team.

Resilience – We stay steady even when challenges arise.

Collaboration – We bring our best when we work together.

Sound like your kind of environment? Let’s dive into the details.

What You’ll Do

  • Support the design of drainage, stormwater, and roadway infrastructure.
  • Assist with quantity takeoffs, engineer’s estimates, roadway geometrics, grading, utility coordination, plan/profile development, and construction details.
  • Perform calculations for accurate and complete design work.
  • Use MicroStation to prepare plans and construction documents.
  • Coordinate with other disciplines and subconsultants as needed.

title: Summer Post-Production Intern
description_html:
Description

About Us:

Weigel Broadcasting Co. is a family-owned media company based in Chicago, Illinois. The company owns and operates national television networks, as well as local broadcast stations throughout the country. Weigel is a leader in broadcast television with MeTV, Memorable Entertainment Television, the number one rated classic TV entertainment network, as well as the Movies! Network in cooperation with the Fox Television Stations, the Catchy Comedy Network, the H&I - Heroes & Icons Network, the Start TV Network and the Dabl Network in association with the CBS Television Stations, the Story Television Network, the MeTV Toons Network in collaboration with Warner Bros. Discovery, and WEST- Western Entertainment Series Television Network. Weigel produces the original network TV programs Svengoolie, Toon In With Me and Collector’s Call. Weigel's local stations include CBS, ABC, The CW, MyNet, Telemundo and Univision network affiliates and independent stations, offering a mix of entertainment programming, local news and professional and college sports broadcasts in 29 U.S. markets including New York, Los Angeles, and Chicago. Weigel is the creator of the nationally syndicated music format MeTV FM. For more information on Weigel, visit: https://www.weigelbroadcasting.com/.

About Weigel Productions

Weigel Productions creates original programming and content for Weigel’s television and digital outlets. Grown out of the long-form productions including Toon In With Me, Svengoolie, Collector’s Call and My Start Story. The Weigel Productions team consists of producers, writers, editors, directors of photography, graphic artists, and researchers.

Post-Production Intern

Weigel Broadcasting in Chicago, IL, is offering an exciting summer internship opportunity for creative college students to gain experience with producing daily television and hands on responsibilities. All interns will receive an hourly rate of pay; Weigel Broadcasting co. does not subsidize housing.

About The Role

  • Work alongside a dynamic production staff
  • Provide hands-on support to both program and post-production staff
  • Rotate between the video editing and graphics department to organize, assist, and create original material
  • Work directly under the guidance of the department heads

What You’ll Do

  • Manage and organize graphics and edit projects
  • Coordinate between editors, writers, graphics team, and producers
  • Complete short video edits for Weigel Productions and studio
  • Research and prepare video and graphic assets within the in-house archive, while utilizing third party archives
  • Deliver finished projects and visual materials within established deadlines
  • Other duties, as assigned

What You’ll Bring

  • Must be able to commit 20 hours per week to the internship
  • Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate degree program
  • Highly organized, detail oriented, and have a passion for visual storytelling
  • Familiarity of Adobe Creative Cloud Suite
  • Premiere Pro and basic editing fundamentals
  • Basic animation fundamentals
  • Motion graphics techniques using Adobe After Effects CC

If you have what we are looking for, please apply with your resume, cover letter, and a link to your demo reel. Within the cover letter please answer the following questions.

  • Why do you want an internship at Weigel Broadcasting?
  • What are your career aspirations and area of interest?
  • What are your expectations for an internship?

What We Offer

  • Location: One block from the aptly named Restaurant Row, & convenient access to highways and public transportation
  • Health & Wellness: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance Package, Long Term Disability Insurance & Accident Insurance
  • Retirement & Savings: HSA Plan, 401k with Company Match
  • Time Off: Vacation/Personal/Sick/Paid Holidays
  • Leave Support: Paid Qualified-FMLA Leave
  • Family Benefits: Paid Parental Leave
  • Commuter Benefits: Tax-free Transit & Tax-free Discounted Parking Access

Weigel Broadcasting Co. maintains an Equal Employment Opportunity Policy for all applicants and employees. We give fair consideration to all qualified persons and afford all our employees opportunities for advancement according to their individual abilities, regardless of race, color, religion, national origin, age or sex, or other protected categories. No opportunities for promotion, transfer or any other benefit of employment will be diminished through discriminatory practices. Employees or prospective employees have the right to notify an appropriate local, state or Federal agency if they believe they have been discriminated against.

Pursuant to state and local pay disclosure requirements, the pay rate/range for this role, with final offer amount dependent on education, skills, experience, and location is $16.60/hour. This role is also eligible for various benefits, including medical/dental/vision, insurance, a 401(k) plan, paid time off, and other benefits in accordance with applicable plan documents. Benefits for Union represented employees will be in accordance with the applicable collective bargaining agreement.

title: UEG Summer Intern
description_html:
We are in relentless pursuit of an equitable and inspiring workplace that is respectful of all, reflects and represents the world in which we live, and fosters trust, collaboration and belonging. Consistent with this approach, we hire the best qualified candidates for all positions.

Role

Summer Interns will support teams across a range of public relations disciplines and contribute to real client work. Interns will gain hands-on experience, build core professional skills, and learn through structured development and mentorship.

Responsibilities May Include But Are Not Limited To

  • Supporting integrated communications and experiential teams across day-to-day client work
  • Assisting with research, audience/cultural insights, and competitive monitoring to inform strategy and creative development
  • Helping build presentations, recaps, and internal/client-ready materials (e.g., decks, timelines, status trackers, event briefs)
  • Supporting planning and execution for events, partnerships, and activations, including logistics coordination and on-site support where applicable
  • Drafting and editing copy for communications materials (e.g., summaries, talking points, social captions, run-of-show notes, attendee comms)
  • Monitoring news, pop culture, and industry trends and sharing relevant takeaways with the team
  • Coordinating with cross-functional partners (creative, strategy, production, social/digital, talent/creator, and project management)
  • Assisting with vendor research, compiling options, and maintaining organized project documentation
  • Participating in brainstorms and collaborating on ideas that align with client objectives and culture moments

Qualifications

  • Interns must be rising seniors or recent graduates
  • A passion for communications, culture, and creating experiences that connect brands to people in meaningful ways
  • Proven organizational skills and the ability to adapt quickly to new assignments, shifting timelines, and evolving priorities
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with attention to detail
  • Collaborative mindset and the ability to work effectively with a variety of people across teams and projects
  • Strong research skills and an ability to synthesize information into clear, actionable takeaways
  • Basic understanding of social and digital platforms (e.g., Instagram, TikTok, X, YouTube) and how audiences engage with content
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Office and/or Google Workspace (PowerPoint/Slides and Excel/Sheets are especially helpful)
  • Demonstrated initiative, curiosity, and a willingness to learn through feedback and mentorship
  • Interest in experiential marketing, sponsorships/partnerships, sports/entertainment, and/or brand storytelling (through coursework, internships, campus orgs, or personal projects)

Plus/nice To Have

  • Familiarity with AI tools and platforms (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini) to support research, content drafting, and trend monitoring
  • Any experience supporting events, productions, campus programming, or client-facing projects
  • Comfort working in a fast-paced environment where details and deadlines matter

Compensation: $21/per hour

Available Departments For Internships

New York

Partnerships (Music, Sports)

Social Strategy

Talent

Communications

Brand Experience

Los Angeles

Partnerships (Music, Entertainment)

LA Office Support

Chicago

Creator

Brand Experience

An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, any contractual agreements, and business or organizational needs. The range listed is just one component of DJEH’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include annual bonuses, a Paid Time Off policy, and region-specific benefits.

DJEH offers a wide range of benefits: medical and dental insurance, vision, 401K, life insurance, disability insurance, paid time off, travel assistance and wellness programing.

DJEH is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and believes in diversity, equity, and inclusion. We seek applications from all qualified candidates without regard to race, color, gender, sex, age, religion, physical or mental disability, military and veteran status, or any other basis protected by federal, state or local law. If you require a reasonable accommodation in any part of the employment process, please let us know.

title: Visual Media Intern
description_html:
Position Title:  Visual Media Intern


Department: Marketing & Communications


Reports To: Associate Creative Director 


Range: $16.75/hr (non-exempt) 




Company Description


At Ravinia, we believe in the transformative power of shared, live-music moments and their ability to inspire individuals, strengthen communities, and foster a more connected world.


Beyond presenting outstanding performances by the world’s greatest musicians, our mission as a not-for-profit organization is to develop broader, more diverse audiences and performers in the music industry with our Reach Teach Play® community engagement and education programs, our Steans Institute’s collaborative and experiential artistic training programs, and more. Together, Ravinia’s initiatives serve tens of thousands of students, families, and emerging professional musicians.


Our 36-acre park is home to North America’s longest-running outdoor music festival and its most artistically diverse. Over 100 different events, featuring a mix of classical, rock, pop, jazz, R&B, indie, and country performers, make up a typical summer season across our open-air and indoor stages, including the annual residency of the Chicago Symphony Orchestra.

Position Summary:

Photo/Video Internship (Summer 2026): The Photo/Video Intern will join Ravinia’s Creative Services team within the Marketing/Communications department, supporting the Ravinia vision through compelling visual storytelling. Reporting directly to the Associate Creative Director of Media Production and the Video Production Manager, the intern will provide hands-on support across all phases of video production, including filming, audio recording, editing, and post-production.

Interns will gain practical experience using professional cameras, stabilization equipment, and lighting techniques to capture the Ravinia experience. They will collaborate closely with fellow interns and members of the Marketing Communications team throughout the production and editing process, developing a well-rounded understanding of professional photo and video workflows in a real-world creative environment. If applicable, academic credit may be available. The ideal dates for the internship are April 2026 - September 2026, dates can be agreed upon based on academic calendars.



Key Responsibilities




Video & Photo Production



  • Produce video content to support branding, sales, marketing, and mission based initiatives.


  • Capture b-roll footage and conduct interviews at Ravinia events to create feature stories throughout the season.


  • Provide support for Ravinia’s photography needs, including concert and event photography.


  • Assist in capturing footage and photos for Ravinia’s mission-based programs, including Reach Teach Play and the Steans Music Institute


On-Set & Technical Support

  • Assist with setting up lighting, cameras, and sound for sit-down and formal interviews.


  • Help set up and light formal interview sets.


  • Utilize professional cameras, stabilization equipment, and lighting techniques to capture high-quality content.


Post-Production & Editing

  • Edit raw digital video footage into polished, finished content using Adobe Premiere Pro.


  • Support post-production workflows to ensure content meets Ravinia brand and quality standards.


Content Collaboration & Creative Development

  • Collaborate with Ravinia’s social media team on content creation and visual storytelling.


  • Work with other interns to conceptualize and execute personal projects that strengthen the Ravinia brand.


  • Collaborate closely with members of the Marketing/Communications team throughout production and editing processes.



   Asset Management & Ravinia



  • Assist with Digital Asset Management (DAM) system, by uploading and tagging photos and videos.



Other duties as assigned




Knowledge and Skills Required



  • Demonstrated knowledge of professional camera and audio equipment with minimal supervision

  • Proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro

  • Outgoing and enthusiastic personality, comfortable approaching the public

  • Developing  organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment

  • Must be able to work nights and weekends

  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality

  • Positive attitude, high energy, and a dedication to teamwork

  • Familiarity with Photoshop, Canva, After Effects, and Bridge is a plus

  • Experience with mirrorless cameras and Ronin stabilizers is preferred






Education and Experience



  • Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from an accredited college, university, or technical program in Film/Video Production, Photography, Media Production, Communications, or a related field.


  • Demonstrated coursework, academic projects, or prior internship experience involving photo and/or video production.


  • Experience operating professional or prosumer camera equipment in an academic, internship, or freelance setting.





Success Factors



  • Learn to safely and confidently operate professional photo and video equipment through training, practice, and supervision.

  • Apply creative direction and feedback to produce photo and video content that meets Ravinia’s quality and brand standards.

  • Follow established production workflows, including file organization, asset management, and use of the DAM system.

  • Demonstrate reliability and professionalism, including punctuality, preparedness, and consistent follow-through on assignments.

  • Show measurable growth in technical and creative skills over the course of the internship.

  • Collaborate effectively in a fast-paced, live-event environment, communicating clearly with supervisors, team members, and the public.




Work Environment


Supportive, creative, and hands-on, with an emphasis on learning, mentorship, collaboration, and professional growth. Primarily this role will report on-site at Ravinia Festival in Highland Park, IL, with occasional off-site or remote editing work as assigned. Work hours vary and include nights, weekends, and outdoor events, particularly during the summer concert season. Work takes place in a mix of office settings, performance venues, and outdoor spaces, requiring flexibility, adaptability, and comfort working in a dynamic fast-paced public-facing environment.




Application Requirements:
Applicants must submit a résumé and links to work samples to be considered.


Benefits


 Eligible for Illinois Paid Leave plan (if employed for 3 months). Concert Pass provided for select shows.






Ravinia is an Equal Opportunity Employer


As an equal opportunity employer, Ravinia strongly believes that diversity of backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives is essential to our success and we strive to create and sustain an equitable and inclusive environment for all our employees. Ravinia does not discriminate on the basis of any protected status under federal, state, or local law. We encourage individuals of all identities and abilities to apply.

title: Artistic Intern
description_html:
REPORTS TO: Chief Artistic Officer

TERM: June-December 2026

HOURS: 25-30 hours per week (In-Office)

Through artistic excellence coupled with innovative, relevant programming; initiatives to connect with and grow new audiences; engagement with communities to ignite creativity across Chicago; and a modernized Opera House that is the setting for truly great experiences, Lyric is leading the advancement of opera. This collaborative vision will deliver ever more exciting, thought-provoking, and inclusive audience and community experiences – while increasing the gravitational pull of our art form, company, and city.

The Artistic Intern will work closely with the Chief Artistic Officer in support of day-to-day executive and administrative operations, including calendar management and general office coordination. In addition, the intern will assist with larger projects such as Artist lookbooks, research, and travel coordination. This is an excellent opportunity for future arts administrators looking to gain work experience at a large arts organization.

DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Chief Artistic Officer Project Support:

  • Support Chief Artistic Officer in administrative functions as it pertains to travel research projects, creating and distributing season lookbooks, maintaining new works tracking log and general administrative support
  • Support Chief Artistic Officer in weekly and season scheduling
  • Generate scripts and welcomes for the Chief Artistic Officer for the upcoming season’s events including, but not limited to, Behind The Curtain Dress Rehearsals, production orientations, Opera Insights, Ardis Kranik Society meetings, General Director Opening Night remarks, and board meetings.
  • Generate and collate Artistic department materials for future grants

Administrative & Office Support:

  • Support the Artistic departments in digital and file organization
  • Attend Artistic weekly team meetings, capturing notes for each meeting, and distributing to participants after each week's meeting
  • Support both the Artistic Operations & Administration and Artistic Planning departments with various audition coordination as available
  • Support Learning Programs for in-person Teacher Professional Development Workshops (PD) Project support for Opera In The Neighborhoods (OIN) tour producing, including venue confirmations, scheduling site visits, the backstage pass (study guide), etc
  • Project support for Opera Pathfinders Youth Program
  • Managing digital migration of Lyric Unlimited documents from Google to SharePoint

Events & Performances:

  • Possibly attend and be involved with select mainstage rehearsals, performances, and Lyric Unlimited offsite events as needed

KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS:

  • Pursuing a graduate degree, preferably in arts administration, music or theatre, or equivalent experience. Recent graduates (up to one year post-graduation) accepted as well.
  • Strong interest in an arts administration career
  • Previous experience in an administrative office environment is preferred
  • Background in opera, theatre, or classical music is required
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Professional, highly organized, and detail oriented
  • Ability to coordinate multiple projects and tasks simultaneously
  • Advanced knowledge of Microsoft and Google Suite including Sheets, Docs, and Google Calendar

Please note this position will be privy to highly confidential information. Must be able to adhere and follow confidentiality requirements as needed.

WORK CONDITIONS:

  • Sitting for extended periods.
  • Ability to operate a computer and to handle other office equipment.
  • Ability to occasionally work evening or weekend functions, deployments, or performances.
  • Fast-paced and dynamic environment.
  • Preferred In-Office Schedule: Monday-Friday (occasional evening or weekend function, time would be adjusted accordingly)

COMPENSATION:

Job Classification: Part-Time, Non-Exempt Intern

Hourly Rate: $16.60

Application deadline: April 9, 2026 (5:00pm CT)

Lyric Opera of Chicago offers intern benefits including professional development and department workshops, complimentary tickets, and other events.

Lyric Opera of Chicago is committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, national origin, sex, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, parental status, socio-economic status, veteran status, or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws. We strongly encourage individuals of all backgrounds to apply.

title: Adult Wellness Center Intern
description_html:
Adult Wellness Center (B.A./BSW-level)


Since 1981, the Adult Wellness Center has offered daily, supervised care to Latino and other older adults.  The program provides a wide variety of fun fitness programs (exercise classes, pedal classes, chair yoga, etc.) and therapeutic activities (music, art, drama, dancing, gardening) to help older adults maintain or improve their emotional, cognitive, and physical well-being. The Center also provides health monitoring, educational workshops, monthly birthday parties, monthly holiday and cultural celebrations, and field trips. The center also offers nutritious meals daily, including breakfast, lunch, and an afternoon snack. Round-trip transportation is also provided based on eligibility. 


 


Population served: Older adults, age 60 and older 


 


Internship learning opportunities: Conduct assessments, provide individual case management support, as well as information and referral to other community services; build one-on-one and small group relationships within a milieu setting; design and coordinate special program activities or projects and conduct educational workshops; participate in program evaluation and performance and quality improvement activities; work with a multi-disciplinary team to identify and support the needs of participating older adults; assist with older adult marketing activities and other initiatives, including networking with community partners. 


 


Internship hours/scheduling available: Monday-Friday 8:30am to 4pm


Requirements:


Must comply with agency background checks, as well as drug and TB testing.


Must be bilingual (English and Spanish).

title: CMN Internship
description_html:
Company Information

For more than 20 years, AEG has played a pivotal role in transforming sports and live entertainment. Annually, we host more than 160 million guests, promote more than 10,000 shows and present more than 22,000 events around the world. We are committed to innovation, artistry, and community, and leverage the power of our 300+ venues, leading sports franchises, marquee music brands, integrated entertainment districts, premier ticketing platform and global sponsorship activations, to create memorable moments that give the world reason to cheer.

Our business is interwoven with the human mind and heart, and we strive to build a diverse and inclusive company that reflects the artists, athletes, and fans that we host; reach beyond traditional boundaries to support the communities in which we operate; and minimize our impact on the environment by adopting sustainable practices throughout our business operations.

If you want to be challenged to up your game and make a difference, then join us in giving the world reason to cheer!

Job Description:

CMN is looking for an intern for our touring department to serve as a key support to CMN’s touring team. This position requires a wide range of administrative knowledge which includes generating and reviewing reports, ensuring accuracy of projects/pending’s before they are front facing, reviewing and managing the departmental Calendar, and communicating with vendors/existing relationships for various tasks. The main goal of the touring intern is to be a direct support to the team throughout current and upcoming tours.

Primary Job Responsibilities:

  • Reviews artwork /spots with precision to ensure accuracy
  • Sales report for recently announced tours
  • Daily ticket sale reports
  • Manage calendar
  • Assisting in the distribution of media artwork assets for different campaigns
  • Ability to forecast and request new graphics for each artist/campaign when appropriate
  • Organize BlueBooks
  • Request avails from venues
  • Create routings for upcoming tours
  • Support the touring department as needed


Requirements

  • Currently pursuing a degree in communications, media, advertisement or other related field
  • Bilingual verbal and written Spanish (required)
  • Excellent organizational skills with the ability to execute projects on time
  • Experience using Excel, Word & PowerPoint
  • Critical thinker with strong conceptual and research skills
  • Strong decision-making skills and attention to detail
  • Comfortable working in high pressure environment with strict deadlines
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team
  • Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and writing skills
  • Basic understanding of budgeting and advertisement skills
  • Ongoing knowledge of the music industry and current music trends


Traveling Requirements

  • None


AEG reserves the right to change or modify the employee’s job description whether orally or in writing, at any time during the employment relationship. AEG may require an employee to perform duties outside their normal description.

Pay Range: $16.60

Bonus: This position is not eligible for a bonus.

Benefits: We offer vacation and sick time under the The Chicago Paid Leave and Paid Sick and Safe Leave Ordinance.

AEG's policy is to hire the most qualified applicants, and we comply with all applicable federal, state and local employment laws in making hiring and employee decisions. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate against applicants or employees on the basis of race, color, marital status, disability, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected status recognized by applicable federal, state or local law.

title: Summer Intern- Shop Operations (Rotational)
description_html:
PRG is the world’s leading technology provider within the entertainment and events industry. Our mission is to bring together the power of our people with our production and rental expertise to deliver unforgettable events.

PRG has the largest inventory of production equipment for hire and offers a complete 360-degree range of services to clients within the live events industry. These include music touring and festivals, TV & Film, Theatre, Sports and E-sports and Corporate live events. Our clients and partners rely on PRG’s innovation, experience and in-depth expertise in video, lighting, rigging, staging, virtual productions, and set & automation systems to bring their creative visions to life. With 38 offices located in North America, South America, Europe, the Middle East, Asia and Australia, PRG offers the ability to provide services worldwide by collaborating with our local partners.

Paid Internships at Production Resource Group are educational work experiences for individuals who wish to explore a career with PRG and/or develop hands-on knowledge and skill in the business of entertainment technology. PRG provides structured programs where interns rotate through the various functions of a particular discipline, giving them a comprehensive overview of the different aspects of the field.

 

The internship program is 12 weeks. The Summer Program runs approximately mid-May to mid-August, specific dates vary.

 

Each Shop Operations Rotational Intern will

  • Gain hands-on experience in the technical and operational aspects of our business.
  • Rotate throughout the departments for cross training requirements of the branch to gain experience in several departments and various equipment. Departments may include Lighting, LED, Audio, Video, Monitors, Screens, Projection, Inbound, Outbound etc., depending on the location.
  • Learn how our operations managers and supervisors manage the workflow,
  • Receive an in-depth understanding of responsibilities throughout the shop based upon position
  • Gain knowledge of production equipment through processes such as check in/out, prepping, kitting, and organizing equipment
  • Participate in projects which may include physical inventory, rack builds, and other client specific requests.
  • If business operations allow, Interns may have the opportunity to work on show site where PRG has installations such as, trade shows, corporate events and film productions.
  • Must be attentive and willing to learn new skills and processes above and beyond their area of expertise.
  • Strong communication skills, motivated and punctual.
  • Previous knowledge, training, schooling, or experience in Audio, Lighting, A/V, Video, LED, Rigging, Media Servers, or other entertainment fields of expertise Is preferred.
  • Ability to work in emails, computers is a must
  • May perform other incidental tasks, as needed.
  • All applicants must be eligible to work in the country where the internship is located.
  • For internships in the US, students must pass a drug test prior to employment.


We are looking for applicants who match ANY of the following

  • Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or higher-level degree program in theatre or entertainment
  • Have experience with technology specializing in any of our disciplines or a complementary field
  • Have received an appropriate degree within the past two years
  • Have appropriate background in the entertainment technology industry


 

Physical demands

  • Must be able to stand or sit for long periods of time
  • Must be able to lift 50 lbs
  • Lifting, carrying, pushing, and pulling objects


The pay for this intern position is estimated to be $18 hr. Actual pay to be determined by the experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities of the applicant combined with location and alignment with market data.

PRG is an Equal Opportunity Employer. PRG is a drug-free work environment, pre-employment testing will be required.

This is a safety sensitive position and will require pre-employment drug screening.

Production Resource Group LLC participates in E-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S.

PRG cares about the safety of our employees and applicants. PRG does not use chat rooms for job searches or communications. PRG will never request personal information via informal chat platforms or unsecure email. PRG will never ask for money or an exchange of money, banking or other personal information prior to an in-person interview. Be aware of potential scams while job seeking. For information on job scams, visit, https//www.consumer.ftc.gov/JobScams or file a complaint at https//www.ftccomplaintassistant.gov/.
description_text:
PRG is the world’s leading technology provider within the entertainment and events industry. Our mission is to bring together the power of our people with our production and rental expertise to deliver unforgettable events.

PRG has the largest inventory of production equipment for hire and offers a complete 360-degree range of services to clients within the live events industry. These include music touring and festivals, TV & Film, Theatre, Sports and E-sports and Corporate live events. Our clients and partners rely on PRG’s innovation, experience and in-depth expertise in video, lighting, rigging, staging, virtual productions, and set & automation systems to bring their creative visions to life. With 38 offices located in North America, South America, Europe, the Middle East, Asia and Australia, PRG offers the ability to provide services worldwide by collaborating with our local partners.

Paid Internships at Production Resource Group are educational work experiences for individuals who wish to explore a career with PRG and/or develop hands-on knowledge and skill in the business of entertainment technology. PRG provides structured programs where interns rotate through the various functions of a particular discipline, giving them a comprehensive overview of the different aspects of the field.

 

The internship program is 12 weeks. The Summer Program runs approximately mid-May to mid-August, specific dates vary.

 

Each Shop Operations Rotational Intern will


Gain hands-on experience in the technical and operational aspects of our business.
Rotate throughout the departments for cross training requirements of the branch to gain experience in several departments and various equipment. Departments may include Lighting, LED, Audio, Video, Monitors, Screens, Projection, Inbound, Outbound etc., depending on the location.
Learn how our operations managers and supervisors manage the workflow,
Receive an in-depth understanding of responsibilities throughout the shop based upon position
Gain knowledge of production equipment through processes such as check in/out, prepping, kitting, and organizing equipment
Participate in projects which may include physical inventory, rack builds, and other client specific requests.
If business operations allow, Interns may have the opportunity to work on show site where PRG has installations such as, trade shows, corporate events and film productions.
Must be attentive and willing to learn new skills and processes above and beyond their area of expertise.
Strong communication skills, motivated and punctual.
Previous knowledge, training, schooling, or experience in Audio, Lighting, A/V, Video, LED, Rigging, Media Servers, or other entertainment fields of expertise Is preferred.
Ability to work in emails, computers is a must
May perform other incidental tasks, as needed.
All applicants must be eligible to work in the country where the internship is located.
For internships in the US, students must pass a drug test prior to employment.



We are looking for applicants who match ANY of the following


Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or higher-level degree program in theatre or entertainment
Have experience with technology specializing in any of our disciplines or a complementary field
Have received an appropriate degree within the past two years
Have appropriate background in the entertainment technology industry



 

Physical demands


Must be able to stand or sit for long periods of time
Must be able to lift 50 lbs
Lifting, carrying, pushing, and pulling objects



The pay for this intern position is estimated to be $18 hr. Actual pay to be determined by the experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities of the applicant combined with location and alignment with market data.

title: Veterinary Extern - General Practice
description_html:
Deerfield Animal Care Center has 3 veterinarians and 4 veterinary assistants

We have ultrasound, in-house lab suite (procyte dx, catalyst 1, IDEXX vet lab UA, IDEXX sedivue DX, IDEXX snap pro, IDEXX coag DX) new digital radiography, dental and surgical suites.

At our hospital, veterinary students and new graduates are welcomed into a supportive, hands-on learning environment guided by a team of compassionate doctors trained in internal medicine, general practice, and integrative medicine. Our veterinarians are passionate about sharing their knowledge, mentoring future colleagues, and helping each learner gain confidence in clinical decision-making and client communication. As a practice in a season of growth and renewal, we value collaboration, accountability, and curiosity—qualities that drive both personal and professional development. We take pride in caring for pets through every life stage and in teaching clients how to do the same, always maintaining a culture of respect, integrity, and teamwork. Together, we’re building not just a stronger hospital, but a brighter future for our patients, clients, and the veterinary profession.

We have a large space, available to support the growth of our doctors and teams. Our hospital offers a diverse range of medical services that reflect our commitment to whole-patient care. One of our veterinarians is trained in integrative medicine, incorporating acupuncture and other holistic techniques to blend Eastern and Western approaches for optimal patient outcomes. We’re also proud to have a board-certified internal medicine specialist with extensive experience in both cardiac and abdominal ultrasound diagnostics, providing advanced support for complex cases. Beyond our clinical focus, we partner with several local rescue organizations and look forward to growing these relationships as our team and hospital continue to expand.

Our hospital is nestled in the vibrant, family-friendly community of Deerfield, where small-town charm meets suburban convenience. The area is filled with great restaurants, cozy coffee shops, and plenty of walking and biking paths perfect for outdoor enthusiasts. We’re just 15 minutes from the shores of Lake Michigan and a quick drive to Ravinia—the renowned open-air music venue that hosts artists and world-class performances, including the Chicago Symphony Orchestra, in a relaxed, picnic-style setting. Whether you’re in the mood for a city adventure or a peaceful weekend getaway, we’re only 30 minutes from downtown Chicago, 40 minutes from the Chain O’Lakes, and about an hour from Milwaukee. It’s an ideal spot for anyone who loves variety—urban energy, natural beauty, and a strong sense of community all in one place.

Externships range from two to eight weeks, depending on student and hospital schedules. All veterinary students (regardless of class year) are encouraged to participate in NVA externships. Housing may be available in some instances.

Veterinary students who are in the clinical portion of their education are eligible for a travel/living expense stipend. Click HERE to view list of stipend amounts and HERE for details on the stipend program. The stipend amount for Deerfield Animal Care Center is $1,500.

title: Development Operations Intern
description_html:
The Development Operations Intern provides support to the Development Department at Ravinia as an important member of the Operations team. This position plays a role in assisting with Ravinia’s annual fund campaign, gift entry and processing, providing exceptional customer service to Ravinia’s 5,000+ individual donors, planning and executing events, and playing a vital role in the VIP Donor Ticketing service, assisting Ravinia’s most generous donors. The position offers an opportunity to gain valuable experience in fundraising, donor relations, and day-to-day operations of a world renowned non-profit arts organization.

Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Provide outstanding customer service and assistance with dining reservations, ticketing, and other requests to all of Ravinia’s donors, during the donor ticket request periods April-June, and throughout the summer.

  • Assist in tracking the Donor Concierge ticket and dining reservation waitlist in a very detailed manner for sold-out concerts.

  • Answer a high volume of phone calls and email inquiries from current and prospective donors during the donor ticket request periods April-June, and throughout the summer. (Phone calls and email can exceed upwards of 75 per day per team member.)

  • Learn and utilize Ravinia’s software platforms to perform various core job functions such as reserving, selling, and exchanging tickets; placing dining reservation requests; processing donations; and tracking waitlists.

  • Represent the Donor Concierge Service in the utmost professional and respectful manner to ensure the highest level of service for Opus and Guarantor donors on the phone, in Ravinia’s restaurants, and throughout the park.

  • Collaborate with the Manager, Donor Relations and Operations and the Manager, Annual Fund Campaign in planning various VIP events including but not limited to tracking event RSVPs, designing invitations, assembling event materials, generating event correspondence, and event execution.

  • Assist the Manager, Donor Relations and Operations with donor research projects aimed at increasing the number of annual donors.

  • Use data in the ticketing system AudienceView to analyze donor benefits usage for highly attended concerts.

  • Update donor records in Ravinia’s CRM (AudienceView) as needed and maintain accurate contact information.

  • Provide assistance to the box office on public sales day by assisting patrons by phone.

  • Assist in concert duty coverage.

  • Special projects and other duties as assigned, including providing administrative support for the Development department as needed, and assisting at various department events throughout the year such as season previews, donor receptions, annual Music Matters benefit, and Women’s Board Gala. 


Qualifications

Knowledge and Skills Required:

  • Superior customer service, communication, and interpersonal skills

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills

  • Capability to multitask and manage multiple projects simultaneously

  • Ability to quickly learn and then utilize various technology platforms

  • Ability to problem solve quickly, calmly, and effectively in a variety of stressful situations

  • Comfortable working with high-level donors face-to-face

  • Excellent listening skills

  • Extremely detail-oriented

  • Capability to work well in a team environment across departments as well as independently

  • Proficient in G-Suite applications

  • Experience working in databases (AudienceView is a plus, but not required)

  • Experience with project management software (Monday.com is a plus, but not required)

  • Proficiency in Zoom Meetings and related video conferencing tools

  • Passion for Ravinia’s mission, particularly music and music education

Education and Experience:

  • Some college or higher preferred

  • Experience in customer service, fundraising position preferred

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of this position.


Additional information

  • Reports to: Development Operations Coordinator

  • This is a full-time position starting in January and running through the end of the season in late September.

  • Must be available to work evenings and weekends outdoors (40+ hours per week) during the summer season.

  • This position can be partially remote, but in-person, in-office work is required (especially on concert days).

  • $16.50 Hourly wage

  • This is a valued position that contributes heavily to the success of the Development Department and Operations team.

title: Production Tech Intern
description_html:
Barstool Sports is seeking a Production Tech Intern to support the Wake Up Barstool team and the CHQ Control Room in our Chicago office. This role offers hands-on experience in live television engineering, control room operations, and broadcast technology for a daily, early-morning sports talk show.

You will work directly with the Wake Up Barstool Director/Technical Director, assisting with daily show setup, live production support, and post-show technical development. This internship is ideal for students interested in live broadcast engineering, technical directing, and production technology.

This internship runs from May 26 to August 28, is based 100% in our Chicago office, and requires an early daily call time of 5:00 AM.

What You’ll Do

  • Assist with daily technical setup for Wake Up Barstool, including studio and control room preparation.
  • Support the Director/TD during live broadcasts as an extra set of hands, eyes, and ears in the control room.
  • Help monitor audio, video, graphics, and streaming elements to ensure smooth live execution.
  • Troubleshoot minor technical issues in real time under supervision.
  • Assist with testing new production workflows, equipment, and technical setups.
  • Collaborate with the production team post-show to brainstorm and help execute creative technical ideas.
  • Support additional large-scale live streams operated out of the CHQ Control Room as needed.

What You Have

  • Rising sophomore, junior, or senior pursuing a degree in Broadcast Production, Television Engineering, Media Technology, Communications, or a related field.
  • Strong interest in live production, control room operations, or broadcast engineering.
  • Basic understanding of studio equipment, signal flow, audio/video systems, or live streaming technology.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to stay calm in a fast-paced, live environment.
  • Willingness to work early mornings and adapt to the pace of daily live production.
  • Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate with on-air talent and production teams.
  • Passion for sports and live media is a plus.

Pay Rate: $16.60/hr

  • THIS INTERNSHIP IS NOT BENEFITS ELIGIBLE*

Barstool Sports is a company that will drive the way media is created and consumed for years to come. Working at Barstool Sports means being part of a team that is solutions-oriented, celebrates creativity, and supports one another.

Joining means having access to competitive and valuable benefits and perks, including but not limited to:

  • Competitive medical, dental, and vision insurance; Flexible Spending Accounts and Commuter Benefits
  • A 4% 401(k) employer match
  • Unlimited vacation time, including Summer break in July and Holiday break in December
  • Monthly employer contribution towards cell phone reimbursement
  • Employee discount to the Barstool Sports store
  • Financial wellness benefits through Origin
  • A monthly stipend for UberEats

What We Value

  • Rep the brand as best you can
  • We have lots of different brains, be open to them
  • Never change who you are, that's who we hired
  • Find the solution, don't be the problem
  • Don't be afraid to try something new
  • Common Sense. Use it.
  • Be there for your audience
  • Always think about how you can help

Barstool Sports is for everyone. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status.

Barstool Sports does not accept unsolicited headhunter or agency resumes. Barstool Sports will not pay any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement.

title: Digital Production Unpaid Internship
description_html:
Digital Production Internship

Unpaid • 4–6 Hours/Week • 3-Month Commitment • Chicago-Based (Hybrid)

PLEASE READ FULL DESCRIPTION BEFORE APPLYING

High Impact Promotion (formerly Salamea.Marketing LLC) is a Chicago-based media and marketing agency specializing in high-impact video production, drone services, event coverage, and content creation for brands, conferences, hotels, and creative partners globally.

We operate with a hybrid creative model, supported by editors, interns, videographers, hosts, and production assistants across multiple time zones—using advanced tools such as DJI drones, Insta360 cameras, Meta Glasses, and our internal multi-device content pipeline.

Our work includes short-form reels, event recaps, interviews, branded storytelling, VR/360 content, livestreams, and digital marketing assets for clients across hospitality, entertainment, fashion, gaming cafés, nightlife, and nonprofit education.

Internship Overview

This is an unpaid, educational internship designed for individuals seeking real-world experience inside a working media production environment.

  • 3-month minimum commitment
  • 4–6 hours per week
  • Hybrid structure (on-site + remote)
  • Portfolio-focused and workflow-driven

This program is ideal for candidates looking to build practical skills, professional experience, and a strong creative portfolio.

Who We’re Looking For

We’re seeking organized, motivated interns who:

  • Want hands-on experience in digital production and media workflows
  • Are reliable, coachable, and open to feedback
  • Are interested in media production, content creation, or digital marketing
  • Want to learn by contributing, not just observing
Key Responsibilities (Digital Production Intern)

Interns support our content pipeline from capture → organization → prep for publishing. Responsibilities may include:

Media Intake & Device Support
  • Import footage from cameras and devices to a central hard drive
  • Help ensure devices are charged, backed up, and ready for production

You may work with:

  • Insta360 (360 video + livestream)
  • DJI Mic (interviews + event recaps)
  • DJI Osmo Pocket 3 (slow motion + event recaps)
  • DJI Mini 4 Pro & DJI Neo (aerial video, photos, livestreams)
  • Insta GO (POV + specialty angles)
  • Ray-Ban Meta Glasses (POV + recap)
  • Portable editing laptops and lighting setups
File Organization & Asset Management
  • Organize footage by event, device, and date
  • Upload and maintain assets in Google Drive following internal structure
Content Prep & Social Support
  • Assist with thumbnails and visual asset selection
  • Support posting across platforms such as LinkedIn and Facebook
  • Draft or refine captions based on brand tone and guidance
What You’ll Receive
  • Hands-on experience with a fast-paced media agency
  • Portfolio projects tied to live client campaigns
  • Professional testimonials & recommendation letters upon completion
  • Networking through 2112 Studios and select Gold Coast events
  • Access to 2112 Studios amenities, including:
  • Content studios & editing bays
  • Production spaces & conference rooms
  • Weekly networking events
  • Free parking
Weekly Culture Perks
  • Sponsored lunches (312 Empanadas)
  • VR soundbath wellness breaks via By The Water Foundation

Top performers receive priority consideration for paid roles after milestone completion.

How to Apply

All candidates must apply through our official intake form:

👉 Apply Here:

https://share-na2.hsforms.com/2xvMZDR95SWiCubdkmY4FPQ41pf6p

Selected applicants will be contacted regarding orientation sessions and next steps.

⭐ Why High Impact Promotion?

Because we don’t simulate the industry — we operate inside it.

title: Mostly Sports Production Intern
description_html:
Barstool Sports is seeking a Mostly Sports Intern to join our team for the summer. Mostly Sports covers the daily world of sports and beyond, blending commentary, internet culture, and fan-driven energy into a fast-growing live show. This role is perfect for someone who lives and breathes sports, understands the digital content landscape, and wants hands-on experience in both live production and long-form content creation.

This internship runs from May 26 to August 28 and is based 100% in our Chicago office.

What You'll Do
  • Assist in the daily production of Mostly Sports, including studio setup, managing technical elements, and supporting smooth live broadcasts.
  • Work closely with the team to expand Mostly Sports’ long-form content output, including vlogs, extended cuts, and live streams beyond the core daily show.
  • Help plan, shoot, and edit additional content initiatives for the summer, with a focus on longer-form storytelling and behind-the-scenes coverage.
  • Clip, edit, and distribute highlights, debates, and viral sports moments across TikTok, Instagram, and Twitter/X.
  • Collaborate with the production team to create engaging, shareable sports content that aligns with the show’s voice and audience.
  • Monitor trending sports news and internet moments to help identify timely content opportunities.
  • Engage with fans across platforms and help drive conversation during and after live broadcasts.
  • Track performance metrics across platforms and identify growth opportunities.
  • Work Monday – Friday, 40 hours per week, in-office, with flexibility for live events and occasional weekend coverage as needed.
What You Have
  • A rising junior/senior with strong passion for sports and deep familiarity with the Mostly Sports brand and tone.
  • Understanding of major sports leagues, storylines, and internet discourse.
  • Demonstrated interest or experience in long-form video content (vlogs, YouTube-style content, live streams, or similar).
  • Proficiency or strong working knowledge of video editing tools (Adobe Premiere, Final Cut, or similar), with an emphasis on longer-form editing workflows.
  • Experience managing social media and driving engagement across Twitter/X, Instagram, and TikTok.
  • Basic graphic design skills (Photoshop or similar) for social and on-air use.
  • Strong organization and multitasking skills in a fast-paced environment.
  • A proactive, self-starting mindset with confidence in a live production setting.

 

Pay Rate: $16.60/hr

*THIS INTERNSHIP IS NOT BENEFITS ELIGIBLE*

Barstool Sports is a company that will drive the way media is created and consumed for years to come. Working at Barstool Sports means being part of a team that is solutions-oriented, celebrates creativity, and supports one another.  Joining means having access to competitive and valuable benefits and perks, including but not limited to:
  • Competitive medical, dental, and vision insurance; Flexible Spending Accounts and Commuter Benefits
  • A 4% 401(k) employer match
  • Unlimited vacation time, including Summer break in July and Holiday break in December
  • Monthly employer contribution towards cell phone reimbursement
  • Employee discount to the Barstool Sports store
  • Financial wellness benefits through Origin
  • A monthly stipend for UberEats
 What We Value
  • Rep the brand as best you can
  • We have lots of different brains, be open to them
  • Never change who you are, that's who we hired
  • Find the solution, don't be the problem
  • Don't be afraid to try something new
  • Common Sense. Use it. 
  • Be there for your audience
  • Always think about how you can help
 Barstool Sports is for everyone. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status.  Barstool Sports does not accept unsolicited headhunter or agency resumes. Barstool Sports will not pay any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement.

title: General Production Intern
description_html:
Barstool Sports is seeking a General Production Intern to join our growing Production team for the summer. This role provides hands-on experience across various aspects of production, assisting on multiple projects simultaneously. You’ll work closely with producers, contribute to content creation, and gain firsthand experience in live events, studio shows, podcasts, and more. This internship runs from May 26 to August 30 and is based 100% in our Chicago office.

What You'll Do
  • Work alongside and shadow Producers on new and existing Barstool Sports productions.
  • Assist with edits and the creation of social clips, ensuring engaging and timely content.
  • Serve as a runner where needed to support on-location shoots.
  • Help set up and break down sets/shoots, ensuring smooth production operations.
  • Support various types of productions, including live events, studio shows, run-and-gun content, interviews, and podcasts.
What You Have
  • A strong familiarity with Barstool Sports content and its unique brand voice.
  • A passion for storytelling and the ability to craft compelling narratives that align with our brand ethos.
  • Strong organizational and collaborative skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment.
  • Experience with Adobe Creative Cloud, particularly Premiere Pro and After Effects.
  • An up-to-date production reel showcasing your work.

Pay Rate: $16.60/hr

*THIS INTERNSHIP IS NOT BENEFITS ELIGIBLE*

title: The Yak Production Intern
description_html:
Barstool Sports is seeking a The Yak Intern to join our team for the summer. The Yak is one of Barstool’s most iconic live shows, known for its off-the-cuff humor, unpredictable energy, wild antics, and deeply loyal fanbase. This role is ideal for someone who understands the chaos, thrives in it, and can help amplify the funniest moments in real time.

This internship runs from May 26 to August 28 and is based 100% in our Chicago office.

 

What You'll Do

  • Assist in the daily production of The Yak, including studio prep, props, tech setup, and live broadcast support.
  • Identify, clip, and edit hilarious moments in real time for rapid distribution across TikTok, Instagram, and Twitter/X.
  • Collaborate with producers to create creative, shareable content that reflects The Yak’s unique tone and personality.
  • Help develop and evolve the show’s voice on social media through creative posts, original graphics, and platform-specific content.
  • Help generate content ideas, bits, and interactive fan moments that fit the show’s humor.
  • Manage live social interactions and help spark viral moments from the broadcast.
  • Track engagement metrics and identify opportunities to grow the show’s digital footprint.
  • Work Monday – Friday, 40 hours per week, in-office, with flexibility depending on show needs and special events. 

 

What You Have

  • A genuine passion for The Yak and a strong understanding of its humor, cast dynamics, and audience.
  • Sharp comedic instincts and an eye for identifying viral moments.
  • Experience with social media engagement and platform-native content creation.
  • An understanding of how to build and maintain a distinct voice on social media through tone, timing, and creative execution.
  • Basic knowledge of video editing (Adobe Premiere, Final Cut, or similar).
  • Basic graphic design skills (Photoshop or similar) for social and on-air graphics.
  • Ability to thrive in a high-energy, unpredictable environment.
  • Strong attention to detail, adaptability, and the confidence to contribute ideas in a fast-moving room.

 

Pay Rate: $16.60/hr

*THIS INTERNSHIP IS NOT BENEFITS ELIGIBLE*

title: Performance Creative Production Intern
description_html:
Black Girl Vitamins is hiring 2 to 3 interns to support high-volume, performance-driven paid social creative.

This role sits at the intersection of design and growth. You will produce and iterate on conversion-focused creative using AI-powered tools to increase testing velocity. The goal is measurable impact on clicks, conversion rate, and revenue.

This is not an AI engineering role. You will use AI tools to accelerate creative production and experimentation.

Ideal for undergraduate or graduate students in Graphic Design, Marketing, Advertising, or Digital Media who want direct exposure to performance marketing.

What You’ll Do

  • Design static paid social ads in 4:5, 9:16, and 1:1 formats
  • Produce multiple creative variations weekly for structured A/B testing
  • Use AI tools to accelerate concept generation, copy variations, and visual iterations
  • Build modular templates for offers, bundles, and promotions
  • Iterate quickly based on performance data
  • Collaborate with the growth team to refine hooks, messaging, and layout

What We’re Looking For

  • Strong design fundamentals: typography, layout, hierarchy
  • Proficiency in Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, or Canva
  • Experience with product or e-commerce creative preferred
  • Comfortable using AI-assisted tools to increase speed and output
  • Curious about what drives clicks and conversions
  • Portfolio required

What You’ll Gain

  • Direct exposure to paid media testing strategy
  • Experience designing for conversion, not just aesthetics
  • Fast feedback loops tied to real performance metrics

Position Details

  • $25 - $30 per hour
  • 10–20 hours per week
  • Chicago-based (remote flexibility available)
  • Immediate start
  • 3–6 month internship (with opportunity to extend)
